main::tutorial_page1_note1_text: Defeat enemies by playing cards from your hand. Cards cost Energy to play. When your energy is spent, end your turn. At the start of your turn, new cards are drawn and Energy is restored to maximum.
main::tutorial_page2_note1_text: Many Defensive cards allow you to gain Block against enemies planning to attack you. Each point of Block reduces physical damage to your Vigour. Half of your Block will decay at the start of your next turn. Mental damage affects your Nerve and cannot be prevented using Block.
main::tutorial_page3_note1_text: During your turn, you can predict an enemy's Intent above them. If the enemy is attacking, hover over them to reveal their target. Allies will share their Intent as well.
main::tutorial_page4_note1_text: Beyond cards, you also have Abilities and Consumables. Each agent has a unique Ability that can be used 3 times per combat. During your journey, you will find Consumables that are removed after use.

main::card_hint_feature_burn_description: After being played, this card goes into the exhaust pile. You may use it once per battle.

main::card_hint_feature_cursed_descripti: This card <color=#ff6e1e>cannot be played</color>. If it remains in hand at the end of the turn, it will play <color=#ff6e1e>automatically</color>.

main::mission_brief_01_01_OldTom: A dark road cuts through the forest to Hangman's Hill, illuminated by the spectral glow of the whispering lichen. It seems to be spreading from here, and you strongly suspect you're close to discovering the source.
main::mission_debrief_01_01_OldTom: The rusty cage crashes open, spilling the remains of Old Tom onto the ground. The whispering lichen's glow dims to nothing, and with its unholy source defeated, it begins to wither. Ridding the forest of this deadly curse should bring relief to the villagers nearby, but the moon's glow is stronger than ever.
main::mission_brief_01_02_Kelpie: You've intercepted a missive from a local constabulary to Scotland Yard. A few officers went missing while on patrol near Blackwell Marsh, and the locals seem to be growing restless.
main::mission_debrief_01_02_Kelpie: The wretched creature falls limp at the side of the pond. As the murky water settles, you can now make out a pile of bones and rags, all that remains of its victims. While the night seems calm, you wonder what other beasts may still lurk nearby.
main::mission_brief_01_03_Herne: A ghostly figure is stalking the Epping Forest paths. The locals are terrified, evoking ancient tales of Herne the Hunter and the Wild Hunt. Myth or menace, it's your duty to investigate.
main::mission_debrief_01_03_Herne: The phantom hunter disappears, leaving behind only rags and the decaying stag skull. You smash the skull and burn the rags, praying that his presence has been banished for good.
main::mission_brief_01_05_Watchers: Something evil haunts the trees surrounding Hangman's Hill. The woods are strange here, the shadows somehow darker, and there have been rumours of a weird creature lurking just out of sight. Several people have gone missing; it's time to investigate.
main::mission_debrief_01_05_Watchers: You triumphed over the highwaymen, at least for now. The shadows are a bit dimmer and the air sweeter, the roads once more safe for travel.
main::mission_brief_01_00_TEMP: TEMP - Go meet three informants and learn what they know.
main::mission_debrief_01_00_TEMP: TEMP - You gathered all the info.
main::spot_card_text_anselina: Visit to gain or replace tarot cards <sprite name="card"> for your mission.
main::spot_desc_anselina: A Roma woman sits cross-legged before a small campfire along the roadside. Though she is old, her eyes are quick and bright. Anselina waves you over to share the warmth. She will also share her guidance and wisdom, for a price.
main::spot_card_text_chapel: Visit the chapel to restore nerve <sprite name="sanity"> or reduce Doom <sprite name="fuel">
main::spot_desc_chapel: Inside the crumbling stone church, you're confronted by three stained-glass saints with hollow eyes. Father Osgood is standing, arms spread, before the altar, muttering to himself in an unfamiliar language. The steeple bell rings out, though the day is perfectly calm.
main::spot_card_text_logging_camp: Visit to scavenge materials for repairing the Starling.
main::spot_desc_logging_camp: The overgrown remains of a logging camp sit abandoned in a broad clearing. You soon notice a glowing lantern in the window of one of the shacks. Perhaps it's not entirely abandoned after all...
main::spot_card_text_manor: Obtain a relic or a powerful <sprite name="card">
main::spot_desc_manor: At the edge of the woods you come upon a faded, ivy-choked manor. The eccentric scholar, Dr. Crowthorne, invites you inside. Within are shelf upon shelf of heavy black tomes, specimens in foggy glass jars, maps and other gewgaws. He offers sanctuary, though the house pulses with an undefinable energy.
main::spot_card_text_rusting_machinery: Visit  to scavenge materials for repairing the Starling.
main::spot_desc_rusting_machinery: You discover a long abandoned machine of unknown purpose. A rusty metal gearbox, chain, and other mechanisms lay half hidden in the weeds.
main::spot_card_text_tavern: Visit to restore Vigour <sprite name="heal">, earn <sprite name="money"> or remove a <sprite name="card">
main::spot_desc_tavern: A deep hush pervades the tumbledown roadside inn, where the patrons stare silently into their cups as the fire struggles against the damp. The innkeeper, Eustace Bell, stands behind the bar, a loaded musket hanging on the wall behind him.
main::encounter_01_01A_description: In a secluded hollow, you discover a small brick cottage. The windows are all boarded over, but a trickle of smoke rises from the chimney. A soft voice calls from behind the door: "Don't look like you have the Whisper yet. You shouldn't be here, it's not safe. Old Tom is watching."
main::encounter_01_01B_description: You follow the lichen's sickly glow up a steep and rocky path. Arriving at the top of Hangman's Hill, you find a sprawling oak tree with a rusty gibbet cage dangling from the sturdiest branch. Within is the corpse of Old Tom. He raises a bony finger towards you, and a malodorous wind rattles the tree.
main::encounter_01_01B_option_fight_resu: The corruption must be stopped at all costs. You stand your ground. Whatever happens, you mustn't let your guard down, lest you shed this mortal coil prematurely.
main::encounter_01_01A_option_talk_resul: "What can you tell me about Old Tom?" A weathered face appears at a crack in the window. "Have you heard of Hangman's Hill? As the legend goes, Tom King was hanged for a highwayman ages back. He's been haunting the place ever since." "Then what about the Whisper, as you call it?" "I reckon it's down to that damned moon somehow. Anyway, I wish you luck." He steps back into the shadows.
main::encounter_01_01A_option_leave_resu: "That path leads to Hangman's Hill. Old Tom is waiting for you."
main::encounter_01_02A_description: You spy something in a foetid pond, which you first take for a hump of rags. A pale woman floats face down at the edge, dress billowing in the still water, hair drifting in a lank fan around her head.
main::encounter_01_02A_option_FishOutThe: You wade through the weeds and attempt to recover the corpse. Suddenly, an unseen force starts to pull you under. You twist your ankle dragging yourself back to land, as a writhing mass of black tentacles bursts up out of the water.
main::encounter_01_02A_option_ApproachWi: The body slips beneath the surface as you approach. For a moment the night is still, then a writhing mass of black tentacles bursts up out of the water!
main::encounter_01_02B_description: Ahead you spot a faint blue light, like some eldritch lamp floating through the swamp, its swaying motion too regular for marsh gas. Whatever it is, it mirrors your steps as you walk, but the dense brush conceals whoever carries it through the gloam.
main::encounter_01_02B_option_FollowTheL: You hear a faint sound on the wind, like children playing. Casting around for the source, it seems to come first from in front, then behind. Without warning, the light blinks out. You wait in the darkness for your eyes to adjust, and discover something stuck in the mud at your feet.
main::encounter_01_02B_option_IgnoreItAn: You come upon a dim lantern sat on a stump by the roadside. The path becomes brighter and rather than follow, the blue light fades. You are left feeling an inexplicable sadness. Still, you must press on.
main::encounter_01_02C_description: Something flickers underfoot, casting outsize shadows that skip and dance by its feeble light. It's a broken lantern among the weeds, its flame guttering but still lit.
main::encounter_01_02C_option_Investigat: Alongside the abandoned lantern, you find a boot half stuck in the mud. You suspect it belongs to one of the missing constables. The moment you touch it, the light winks out.
main::encounter_01_02C_option_MoveAlongF: A broken lantern sounds like shorthand for danger in these parts. You quickly continue past and put it out of your mind.
main::encounter_01_03A_description: A gap in the undergrowth, a recently snapped twig, and flattened grass, suggests an animal has passed this way. Perhaps a deer, perhaps something else.
main::encounter_01_03A_option_FollowStag: You part a hanging mass of ivy to reveal a moonlit glade. There stands a majestic white stag, glowing faintly in the moonlight. Walking away, it pauses to glance back as if inviting you to follow. It fades from sight like an apparition as it reaches the line of trees, but your attention is drawn to something in the grass.
main::encounter_01_03A_option_KeepOnStag: You shiver as a chill wind passes over you. A heavy gloom seems to have fallen upon the forest here, but you resolve to move forward.
main::encounter_01_03B_description: Someone marked the trail here long ago, blazing a yew tree with a crude symbol—a broken longbow. At the foot of the tree is a small collection of objects, perhaps offerings left by travellers.
main::encounter_01_03B_option_LeaveOffer: You search your bag and choose a small item to leave with the other trinkets. The woods are no less ominous, but you feel slightly more at ease.
main::encounter_01_03B_option_TakeOfferi: The stuff is mostly junk, of significance to someone perhaps, but not you. You decide to take a coin purse. Counting out the money within, you feel as though something is watching you.
main::encounter_01_03B_option_BackAwayFr: You don't know who or what this offering is meant for, but you know to leave it well enough alone.
main::encounter_01_03C_description: You come upon a pile of skulls, stacked up like a high stone cairn. The mist thins and a figure is revealed: he carries an axe made of bone, a stag's skull atop his shoulders in place of a man's head, its eyes aglow with hellfire. Herne the Hunter is here, and you are his prey.
main::encounter_01_03C_option_FaceHunter: Instead of fleeing as the monster might expect, you stand your ground, the initial moment of terror wrestling with your newfound resolve.
main::encounter_01_04A_description: You can barely believe your eyes—a swatch of moonlight seems to mark out the forest path. You step out of the shadows and back onto the well-travelled road.
main::encounter_01_04A_option_LeaveFores: As the darkness dissipates in the moonlight, you emerge from the woods, thankful that tonight won't be your last in this world.
main::encounter_01_04B_description: The path forks where a bulbous, lightning-struck tree looms overhead. Its splintered trunk seems to reach out to the stars like spindly fingers. From the left comes the sound of running water, from the right distant birdsong. Otherwise, all is still and silent.
main::encounter_01_04B_option_HeadToWate: The branches hang so low here that you're forced to crawl until finding a small waterfall and a creek. Though you dare not drink the water, the creek may lead to an exit.
main::encounter_01_04B_option_FollowBird: There's a decent path here, and you follow it for a while. The woods seem very quiet, and you feel as though you're being watched. A branch snaps as something leaps from the undergrowth.
main::encounter_01_04C_description: The dead trees lean close, hemming you in, the overlapping branches obscuring the moon's light. The path you were following fades into a thicket of sharp thorns, forcing you to turn back.
main::encounter_01_04C_option_TurnBackFr: You're left with no choice but to retrace your steps. The atmosphere grows still more oppressive, and you find yourself panting for breath. You're losing time, and with it any hope of making it out of this place alive.
main::encounter_01_05A_description: The branches overhead cast long and warped shadows in the pale moonlight. A long-dead tree, blackened by fire, seems oddly out of place.
main::encounter_01_05A_option_Investigat: Though the branch is plainly bare, it casts a shadow of a hanging noose. The rope seems to sway, though there is no wind.
main::encounter_01_05A_option_KeepWalkin: You stumble along in a daze, and eventually come to notice several pairs of eyes, shimmering in the scant light as they watch you from either side. But when you startle and turn, there's nothing there. The longer this night lasts, the more it seems your mind is playing tricks on you.
main::encounter_01_06A_description: On the outskirts of town, you find the crumbling remnants of the town well. It pulses faintly with an eerie violet light. From the depths comes a low hum.
main::encounter_01_06A_option_PeerIntoDe: Strips of lichen spread aggressively up the sides of the well like ivy. You hold your breath against the violet miasma rising from below. This must be how the Whisper has been spreading to the farmers.
main::encounter_01_06A_option_DropStone_: You drop a pebble into the well to probe its depth. Instead of a splash, the void returns a shrieking whistle that stings your ears. As the echo fades, you register the sound of breaking branches in the trees behind you.
main::encounter_01_06B_description: You come upon a small farm, encircled by a strip of scorched earth. Within lies a dead cow, ritualistically disemboweled. Its eyes glow faintly.
main::encounter_01_06B_option_ExamineCor: Tendrils of purple lichen spew from the belly of the cow like snakes fleeing a fire. As you approach, the animal appears to still be moving despite its tortured state. You think to put it out of its misery, but instead decide to keep your distance.
main::encounter_01_06B_option_LeaveSite_: The scene you're witnessing strikes you as a warning. You heed it and swiftly leave, before those behind the ritual return and take an interest in you.
main::encounter_01_07A_description: In the middle of a small hamlet, a few maddened villagers lurk with sling blades and other makeshift weapons, howling with devilish delight. You can see the well, but you may not be able to reach it without a fight.
main::encounter_01_07A_option_AttemptSte: The lunatics are distracted, allowing you to reach the well. You toss a rope down into the darkness and call down that you've come to help. A weak "God bless you!" rises from below. However, this draws the attention of the lunatics in the square, who turn as one in your direction. They're going to attack.
main::encounter_01_07A_option_MeetThemBr: "Come on, then!" you shout. They laugh maniacally and begin to charge.
main::encounter_01_08A_description: A low, even sound seems to rise from the ground beneath your feet. Casting about for the source, you realise it comes from the flowers. Somehow, they are humming in unnatural unison.
main::encounter_01_08A_option_CoverEars_: You block out the song, but it still leaves your head spinning. As you stumble past, you find something half-buried in the dirt.
main::encounter_01_08A_option_ListenToSo: There is a nagging familiarity about it, like a children's nursery rhyme. As you listen, you succumb to an inexplicable urge to sing along—though you cannot fathom the language—about realities yet to be and a world doomed to die.
main::encounter_01_08B_description: One of the pods is animated, tilting and wobbling on the spot. As you approach, a drawn-out groan startles you. It's a voice, a person, calling out from beneath the pod's pulsating surface!
main::encounter_01_08B_option_LeaveThemR: You come to the unsettling realisation that the pods must harbour some of the missing people. But there are so many. You do your best to put it behind you and move on.
main::encounter_01_08C_description: The pods all sprout from a web of thick purple vines. Following the vines, you discover that they all lead deeper into the forest, as if the pods were the nodes of some unholy nexus.
main::encounter_01_08C_option_CutVines_r: The pods in Rupert's Hollow fall silent, and the purple glow fades. If anything lived inside them, it's at peace now.
main::encounter_01_08C_option_LightVines: The fire spreads rapidly, consuming the pods, which burst like popped corn. One by one, bodies—human bodies—burst from the conflagration like seeds and thump to the ground. The sight and smell are seared in your memory.
main::encounter_01_09A_description: You follow a river to an old sawmill. Hastily made barriers and spiked pallisades slow your approach, suggesting it was used as some sort of redoubt. Multiple Enthralled are impaled on the barricades, but the camp is now quiet. The dirt path continues up the hill.
main::encounter_01_09A_option_Investigat: You pry the boards from the door and call out, hoping someone may have taken refuge within. But from the dim light of the shack's interior rise the glowing eyes of more Enthralled, who race towards you in a primal rage.
main::encounter_01_09A_option_FollowSawm: You continue up the path. Though there are numerous bodies of the Enthralled here, you find no sign of the living.
main::encounter_01_09B_description: In a clearing at the edge of the path, a crude wooden effigy—a wicker man—stands unfinished. With the approach of the spreading lichen, the workers never had the chance to light it. The corrupted bodies of several workers are caged within. Perhaps they hoped this sacrifice would protect them.
main::encounter_01_09B_option_BurnWicker: You set the wretched thing ablaze with a torch, covering your mouth with your sleeve as the flames consume it. Perhaps the deed will bring some measure of comfort to the souls who died here.
main::encounter_01_09B_option_MoveOnFrom: With an effort, you drag your eyes away from this new horror, telling yourself there's nothing more to do here. You'll leave it to the authorities to clean up the mess.
main::encounter_01_10A_description: In a field beside the old manor, you find a crudely carved statue, several meters tall. It appears to have been hewn from a single black stone and must be tremendously heavy.
main::encounter_01_10A_option_ExamineSta: Some of its features are reminiscent of a fertility goddess, but the longer you try to focus on its form, the less recognisable it becomes.
main::encounter_01_10A_option_Investigat: Half concealed in some nearby brambles, you find a discarded satchel containing a sheaf of correspondence. You startle at the sound of a splintering branch, but can make nothing out in the darkness. Gathering up the papers, you hasten on.
main::encounter_01_10B_description: The crowd cheers, or screams—impossible to discern which. When, for a moment, the moon's glow fades, they exchange bewildered glances before the sky flushes scarlet again. You slip away before you can be spotted.
main::encounter_01_11A_description: Off the forest path, you find a secluded glade that seems to be free of the purple moss. The moonlight gleams silver through the leaves, and you enjoy a moment of peace. At your feet, a neat ring of toadstools glistens with dew.
main::encounter_01_11A_option_EnterRing_: You're startled by the sound of bells. "We know why you are here, but the girl is ours now." The voices speak in unison, though you cannot see the source. "The Whispering Lichen will come to control these woods. Leave now, you will get no further warning." The wind calms, and you sense you are alone again.
main::encounter_01_11B_description: You stop to rest by an oak tree, and your sleep is disturbed by a vision. You stand before a stone table, upon which rest three swaddled infants. One is blanketed in white linen with a lilac pattern. The second is wrapped in muslin with bluebirds. The third is swaddled in green cloth embroidered with tiny shepherds. Black water is rising at your feet, and you know you must choose one of them to save.
main::encounter_01_11C_description: You return the girl to her parents. As you enter the home, the thing masquerading as their daughter confronts you. With a face disfigured and pale, it declares, "You wretched people have only doomed yourselves!" The family cowers and awaits your decision.
main::encounter_01_Handcart_description: You see a pair of raggedy-dressed men pushing a handcart, their cargo covered in tattered canvas. One of them is hunched over to keep the wheel aligned while the other pushes. Both are cursing in distress when they spot you.
main::encounter_01_Handcart_option_Offer: "Good evening, gentlemen, are you in need of a hand?" One of them pauses in his toil to reply, "Unless you’ve got a spare axle about you, you're no use to us. But here, my brother Jacob—may God keep him—left me this and I have no use for it."
main::encounter_01_SicklyWoman_descripti: In a woodsy glade you discover a young woman, her flesh crawling with lichen, her eyes dim and unfocused as she mutters to herself. Though you can barely make out the sounds, you somehow grasp their meaning. "Leave," she whispers. "Save yourself while you still can."
main::encounter_01_SicklyWoman_option_Tr: You sit the woman down and give her some water. "I fled here as this purple rash covered my body, and thoughts of rage and murder flooded my mind. I expect I shall either expire or become something new. If you somehow make it back, give the Bitterby family my regrets and tell them not to search for me. I am at peace." You watch as she slowly draws her last breath. For a moment, she is still, but then the corpse begins to convulse. She's one of them now.
main::encounter_01_SicklyWoman_option_Es: Her mumbled warning is more than enough for you. You flee along the path, hands stretched to fend off the hanging branches. Whatever the thrashing sounds at your back presage, you do not know.
main::encounter_01_Handcart_option_Inter: "What's that you're hiding?" you demand. The men are both quick to anger. "It's a sodding body, all right? Our brother Jacob caught the Whisper yesterday, and Lord did it take him fast. Let us carry him from this cursed place in peace." The other man adds with some remorse, "A word of warning: best not travel alone out here. Old Tom haunts these woods, and folks say he's to blame."
main::encounter_01_Handcart_option_Forci: "What have we here?" Pulling back the rags reveals the corpse of a young man. His face and arms are covered with a purple, mossy growth. You stagger back, the sight and smell making you retch. Gesturing your apologies, you flee into the night.
main::encounter_01_08B_option_CutThemLoo: The pod cracks open, releasing a cloud of noxious spores. When you recover from a coughing fit, you find a farmer slumped on the earth, his form covered with whispering lichen. He turns to you angrily.
main::encounter_01_MarshMill_description: Wading through the acrid waters, you see a mill perched atop a grassy patch of land in the middle of the forest. Emerging from the reeds, you discover an emaciated corpse slumped against the front door, a cloth pouch clenched in one of its fists. The word "No" is scrawled on the door in what appears to be dried blood.
main::encounter_01_MarshMill_option_Sear: You work with both hands to pry apart the rigidly clenched fingers. Eventually, his digits snap free, and you extract the pouch. As you inspect the contents, something snarls from behind the mill's door and begins to bang against it. You dash away before tempting the Fates any further.
main::encounter_01_MarshMill_option_Open: You shove the body aside and open the door to the stench of death. A heavy revolver lies on the hay-strewn floor. When you pick it up and check the rounds in the chamber, something creeps out from a mound of hay on the far side of the room. Your heart bangs in your chest—an Enthralled steps into the moonlight, its fiery gaze trained upon you.
main::encounter_01_MarshMill_option_Leav: Preferring not to share the dead guardian's fate, you set aside your curiosity. The mill will remain a mystery.
main::encounter_01_ChapelNighttime_descr: The floorboards creak with every step inside the chapel, once a sanctuary to the Lord's messengers and those seeking His blessings. A broad plank bridges a hole between the pews, upon it the body of a priest, his arms dangling down over the gap. Two small bags are tied to his wrists.
main::encounter_01_ChapelNighttime_optio: You untie the knot on the right wrist, and the body starts to shift to its left. You tear the bag from its wrist and leap back. The planks splinter apart, and the body drops into the darkness with a terrific crash. You push down your terror and inspect the bag's contents.

You untie the knot on the left wrist, and the body starts to shift to its right. You tear the bag from its wrist and leap back. The planks splinter apart, and the body drops into the darkness with a terrific crash. You push down your terror and inspect the bag's contents.

Under the watchful eyes of the Lord, you know better than to loot a house of worship. As you exit, you study the chapel's ruined facade, saddened by this holy structure's decay in an increasingly wicked world.
main::encounter_01_DingyTavern_descripti: The reek of alcohol and tobacco floods your nostrils as you enter a pub filled with seedy wanderers. As you take a seat at the bar, the portly and bug-eyed landlord approaches with two tin jugs. "Only drinkers are welcome here. It's ale or lager tonight, what will it be?" he says.
main::encounter_01_DingyTavern_option_As: The landlord sets a jug on the bar top. "This un's stale, so it's on the house," he says. You force yourself to drink, even as the musty contents burn your throat and guts. A small enough price to drown out the horrid sights and sounds you've recently witnessed.

The landlord sets a jug on the bar top. "This un's stale, so it's on the house," he says. You force yourself to drink its musty contents, even as your head starts aching. A small enough price to numb the pain you've endured lately.
main::encounter_01_WellRuins_description: You find some ruins off the forest path, and a well next to an archway. The only things moving before you are the swaying bucket above the well and the eddying leaves within the archway. Curiosity and tension gnaw at your mind.
main::encounter_01_WellRuins_option_Insp: You plunge your hand into the bucket. For a moment, you grasp nothing but water, then something bumps against your fingers. You pull the item free as the thread holding the bucket breaks. You inspect your find as the bucket tumbles to the bottom of the well.
main::encounter_01_WellRuins_option_Inve: In the dim light, you see only stone blocks split by growing vegetation. Then you spy something glittering in the moonlight. You pick it up before the sound of the bucket crashing down the well makes you jump. You steal into the night before the sound can attract anything from the woods.
main::encounter_01_WellRuins_option_Leav: You've had enough of ghastly encounters in abandoned places. You return to the path, glad for its relative safety.
main::encounter_01_GladeRest_description: Both your limbs and eyelids grow heavy as you force your way through the woods. You long for a moment's rest. Temptation strikes you as you come across a bit of clear ground where the lichen is at least an arm's length away.
main::encounter_01_GladeRest_option_Rest: You sit at the base of a tree and close your eyes. Perhaps the lichen is inching closer to you, but you're too tired to worry about it. When you wake, it is all but touching you. You dash away from that cursed place.
main::encounter_01_GladeRest_option_Leav: You give your head a shake and pick up the pace. The farther you go, the more the lichen retreats, but you hurt yourself struggling through a dense patch of brambles. You emerge from the woods with most of your sanity intact, but physically wounded.
main::encounter_01_MushroomPath_descript: Oversized mushrooms and thorny undergrowth make way for a lantern-lit fork in the road. You have no idea which path to take, nor the horrors that may await you.
main::encounter_01_MushroomPath_option_G: This path has fewer mushrooms, but further on, fewer lanterns as well. The darkness makes you doubt your decision. Eventually, you return to the main forest path, but the toll on your mind is unmistakable.

There are more mushrooms here, and also more lanterns. However, the fungi's toxic spores are spreading. The forest is trying to weaken you. Though you emerge from the forest in one piece, some part of you still needs to recover.
main::encounter_01_MoonlightClearing_des: You step into a clearing brightly lit by the looming moon. A small field to your left is lit by a crackling campfire. You know better than to loiter in the woods, but you would like to bask in at least one of these light sources, for luck. A cabin in the distance also piques your curiosity.
main::encounter_01_MoonlightClearing_opt: You study the moon's purple effulgence. Disturbing thoughts grow faint as you marvel at the earth's lonely satellite. You take a deep breath and walk on.
The fire washes away the chills of the woods. Mankind's forebears sought comfort from flames just like these. You depart feeling emboldened.

You approach and open the door with a strained creak. The first thing that catches your attention is a small trinket that you happily pocket. But when you make to leave, a young wolf leaps out to take a bite at your thigh. You smash a fist down on its head, and it retreats into the shadows with a low growl.
You'd rather not idle about where any wicked creature might see you. Leaving the clearing, you also leave your apprehension behind.
main::encounter_01_LichenPath_descriptio: The lichen continues uninterrupted as you traverse the forest. The one natural bit of life around you is a magpie struggling to free itself from some branches above. There are two odd-looking items nearby: one resting upon some leaves on a lower branch, and another inside a hole in the tree trunk.
main::encounter_01_LichenPath_option_Tos: Toss a Pebble at the Leaves
The item is jostled from its perch and falls into your hands. The magpie shakes itself free, snatches the other item in its beak, and flies off into the gloom.
main::encounter_01_LichenPath_option_Get: You shimmy up the tree and, with the prize in hand, leap down. The magpie shakes itself free, snatches the other item in its beak, and flies off into the gloom.
main::encounter_01_LichenPath_option_Lea: The magpie is too high up for you to assist. Besides, there are fiends in the dark who will not scruple to attack the idle, so you move on your way. Hopefully, the bird will break free before it becomes some other thing's quarry.
main::encounter_01_AbandonedFarm_descrip: You stumble upon a farm, its occupants long fled from the disease afflicting the flora and fauna, including the livestock. As uninviting as the place is, curiosity beckons you towards two structures.
main::encounter_01_AbandonedFarm_option_: You open the barn's doors and cover your nose against the reek of manure. Something's poking out of a mound of hay. You retrieve the item and exit the barn.
You search every room, but find nothing of interest until you come upon an open chest in the cobwebbed attic. Grabbing its contents, you make your exit.
Something tells you the owners weren't likely to leave anything valuable behind. You walk past the farm, hoping to reach civilisation eventually.
main::encounter_01_ForestCamp_descriptio: Emerging from the woods' darkest recesses, you spy a caravan in a clearing. Close by are a tent and a table, but the site is deserted. A warm campfire suggests that someone was recently here, though you have no desire to wait for them in a forest full of predators.
main::encounter_01_ForestCamp_option_Loo: The inside is a chaotic mess, books and knick-knacks scattered everywhere. You pocket a lone item from the shelf to your right and move on.
main::encounter_01_ForestCamp_option_Ins: Upon the table are a crystal ball, a candle, and several sheets of paper. A gust of wind sends a few sheets flying to the floor, revealing an item you hope no one will miss. You take it and leave the peculiar premises.
main::encounter_01_ForestCamp_option_Wal: As tempting as it may be, right now you'd prefer someplace less mysterious. You leave, hoping the residents are able to return before the wilderness claims them.
main::encounter_01_10B_option_ProcessWit: Reflect on What You Just Witnessed
You try to make sense of the sky's shifting colors and the mad folks' delirious chorus. Ultimately, you're left with more questions than answers about what just transpired.
main::encounter_01_10B_option_WalkAway_r: You step quietly away, lest you become embroiled in what can only be described as unearthly madness. The cheers—shrieks, more like—from the crowd still leave you shaken, however.
main::encounter_01_11A_option_LeaveRingB: Whatever the mushrooms represent, you want no part of it. You put some distance between yourself and the fungi, hoping to preserve what's left of your sanity.
main::encounter_01_11B_option_SaveLilac_: You reach for the bundle with the lilac pattern, before the rising water swallows everything in sight. Upon waking, you realise there's nothing in your hands. Suddenly, a branch breaks behind you, and a young girl emerges from the woods. Without a word, she hands you a gift, and you offer to help her find her way home.
main::encounter_01_11B_option_SaveBluebi: You reach for the bundle with the bluebird pattern, before the rising water swallows everything in sight. Upon waking, you realise there's nothing in your hands. Suddenly, a branch breaks behind you, and a young girl emerges from the woods. Without a word, she hands you a gift, and you offer to help her find her way home.
main::encounter_01_11B_option_SaveShepar: You reach for the bundle with the shepherd pattern, before the rising water swallows everything in sight. Upon waking, you realise there's nothing in your hands. Suddenly, a branch breaks behind you, and a young girl emerges from the woods. Without a word, she hands you a gift, and you offer to help her find her way home.
main::encounter_01_11C_option_Glare_resu: The creature gradually begins to change form, melting into the shape of a weasel with scaly, black wings. It screeches at you before flying into the woods.
main::encounter_01_11C_option_HandGirl_r: Swiping at the creature, it dodges and bolts away from the house, screaming out a warning before fading into the woods. You sense there's something to the changeling's warning, but tell yourself to accept that some things remain a mystery.
main::mission_brief_01_04_DarkWoods: Beneath the light of the crimson moon, Epping Forest seems corrupted somehow. The trees are twisted into unnatural shapes, and you feel as if you're being observed by a lurking evil. The path you were following has come to an end. You'll have to retrace your steps.
main::mission_debrief_01_04_DarkWoods: Nobody wants to be lost in the woods even under normal circumstances, and the endless night made matters much worse. You can only hope that you won't lose your way again.
main::mission_brief_01_07_TheWell: A carrier pigeon delivered a message to the Starling. "Bloodthirsty faces in the night forced me to take refuge in the old town well. If this message reaches you, I can only survive here a few more days. Please help."
main::mission_debrief_01_07_TheWell: Following the old man's feeble cries, you toss a rope down into the darkness and haul him up. He's filthy and emaciated, but he will recover in time. He thanks you profusely for your aid.
main::mission_brief_01_09_TheRot: A logging camp in the woods has gone silent, and a rancid wind now wafts from that direction. The workers' loved ones are calling for someone to investigate and uncover the loggers' fate.
main::mission_debrief_01_09_TheRot: With the woodsman laid to rest, you return to their families. They are heartbroken, but grateful to have closure. You warn them not to stray far from home until this chaos has passed.
main::mission_brief_01_06_InTheWater: The farmers north of the forest are complaining about a weird phenomenon: the water from their wells is infused with a ghostly purple glow. These same folks were last seen dancing wildly around a fire, a murderous rage lighting their eyes.
main::mission_debrief_01_06_InTheWater: You stand over the final Enthralled as its body spasms and seizes, then crumbles into spores and dust. The well water still glows, and the soil hums softly beneath your boots. The source may be deeper yet—but for now, the fields have fallen silent.
main::cutscene_00_01_a: Londoners are no stranger to a thick fog, HUGO, but this is no regular weather phenomenon.
main::cutscene_00_01_b: Shall I recap? First, the fog lifted and we could see the moon, its natural procession halted, with that unnatural violet hue. Then, random violence erupted in the streets, and crazed animals. Even for the East End that's unexpected. But where did they come from? And now, reports of radical zealots causing mayhem.
main::cutscene_00_02_b: Well, it's no mystery why Her Majesty chose to enlist my aid. I am after all the brightest technological mind of our era.
main::cutscene_00_02_c: Yes, thank you, HUGO. I believe we'll find the answer in the skies above London. Take a look at the Aetheroscope. These radiation readings... Well, I don't know what to make of them. If the equipment is functioning properly, it shouldn't be possible.
main::cutscene_00_04_a: The engines must have been damaged in the storm. Fortunately my armour kept me safe, but we'll need to make some repairs.
main::cutscene_00_04_c: HUGO, I believe these locals intend to do us harm. Kindly relay a missive to the Syndicate dispatch. We may need some assistance.
main::cutscene_00_05_a: Miss Molly, looks like you have correspondence, it must be urgent to reach you here.
main::cutscene_00_05_b: I won't be needing that room tonight, Eustace. I'm off to help an old friend.
main::cutscene_00_05_c: Alright Hercules, quit worrying that soup bone and come on.
main::cutscene_00_06_a: She'd make a fine barn, Grizwald.
main::cutscene_00_06_b: The Starling's engines took a beating in the storm, but she'll fly again. I just need the right parts.
main::cutscene_00_06_c: The two of you seem to have your hands full. What exactly does Her Majesty need from me?
main::cutscene_00_06_d: Our Engines need replacement parts. Not something you can pick up in the ha'penny shoppe, but I'm sure you'll manage. HUGO and I need to stay here and finish repairs.
main::cutscene_00_06_f: I'm certain the lunatics who accosted us outside were motivated by some external force. If you're able, find out what it is and put a stop to it.
main::cutscene_01_01_a: The Aether you scavenged should be sufficient to get us in the air and back to London. But first, I suggest we set course for the industrial area north of the city.
main::cutscene_01_01_b: If the radiation coming from the moon is somehow connected to the production of Aether, the factories would be a good place to start our investigation.
main::cutscene_01_01_c: You're right, HUGO, we should request more help from the Syndicate. It certainly does look like we're going to need it.
main::travel_card_battle_tensions_descri: Battle with humans driven mad by the Chaos Moon
main::travel_card_battle_veil_descriptio: Battle with creatures from another world
main::encounter_00_01A_description: You see a pillar of smoke and follow it to a small farm, where the Starling rests in a field. Despite signs of a struggle, it looks like lights are on inside.
main::encounter_00_01A_option_enter_resu: You climb the gangplank and find Grizwald inside, ratcheting a leaking pipe. He looks relieved to see you, and invites you to join him at a table covered in maps and scientific charts.
