main::mission_objective_02_01_ProfessorV: Put an End to Professor Vayne's Experiments
main::mission_brief_02_01_ProfessorVayne: Rumour has it that a certain Professor Vayne has been less than ethical in his experiments lately, experiments that have been let loose to torment the residents of Shoreditch. His lab can be found in a disused factory.
main::mission_debrief_02_01_ProfessorVay: Vayne will no longer conduct his dark experiments. Yet, in the aftermath of the destructive battle, you see a hulking specimen break free of a glass capsule and leap from a small window into the night. The horror, it seems, shall continue.
main::mission_brief_02_02_TheAbomination: A witness reports a grisly murder on factory grounds and summon you to investigate. When you arrive, you are shown evidence of a gargantuan creature. The destruction left in the Abomination's wake is scarcely to be believed, but at least the splintered doorframes and clawed-up streets will make the thing easy to track. You're less sanguine about actually killing it, but such is the job.
main::mission_debrief_02_02_TheAbominati: The Abomination's breath is reduced to a laborious wheeze as it extends its mutant claw in a final supplication. Then its arm falls to the ground with an unceremonious thud. The ordeal is over. You can't help but wonder what sort of creature it was before the Professor doomed it to this sorry existence.
main::mission_brief_02_03_TheSilverCoin: You receive an urgent letter from a gentleman named Alphonse: "I humbly request the presence of Her Majesty's Finest to investigate troublesome reports of a spreading disease. Please come to Caen Wood Manor, observing the utmost discretion." It's sealed with a wax insignia resembling a coin.
main::mission_debrief_02_03_TheSilverCoi: As the cursed Twins slump to their knees in defeat, they release a final, harrowing howl that tapers into stillness. Their bodies remain lupine, masking their human identities in death. They threaten the streets of London no more.
main::mission_brief_02_04_APlaceOfWorshi: A church in Hampstead has been beset by horrific creatures. The terrified parishioners have taken refuge within the sacristy and sealed the door, but the barricade won't last long.
main::mission_debrief_02_04_APlaceOfWors: The unholy creation sputters and collapses to the floor, the rattle of the stained glass echoing its final breath. The parishioners rush out from their confinement with much mopping of brows and expressions of relief. This threat, at least, has been quelled, but the creator of these monstrosities must still be dealt with.
main::mission_brief_02_05_TheBodySnatche: A spate of grave robberies in the north of London has led to wild speculations among the locals. As more exhumations are discovered, townsfolk are saying the dead walk the streets.
main::mission_debrief_02_05_TheBodySnatc: These ruffians were extremely motivated in their grisly mission, but to what end? You discover a note tucked away in one of their pockets: "Twenty bodies are needed for this evening. Remember, the Professor wants fresh meat, the recently deceased only. Fail to meet your quota and you will join them."
main::mission_brief_02_06_AnarchyByMoonl: A mob of werewolves has been terrorising the streets. These beasts are attacking all in their path and have been reported to carry red flags with anarchist symbols.
main::mission_debrief_02_06_AnarchyByMoo: Even when losing all control, vukasin are never this savage, seemingly pursuing cruelty for their own sake. You've no doubt that the blood moon is behind this increased ferocity and vitality.
main::mission_brief_02_07_OneThousandMou: An infestation of rats has spiraled out of control. People fear they are rabid, their eyes aglow and their chittering uncanny.
main::mission_debrief_02_07_OneThousandM: These weird priests seem to worship the rats like gods. However, you doubt they were the ones in control. Someone or something must be directing their actions.
main::mission_brief_02_08_FailedExperime: A mob of angry workers is throwing stones at a man outside a burning factory. As you approach, you mark the copper piping and mechanisms that have somehow given him life.
main::mission_debrief_02_08_FailedExperi: Discovering an abandoned narrowboat along the River Lea, you send the wretched fellow on his way. "You've shown a great kindness that I shan't soon forget. I can manage on my own now, but I shall never know real peace until the Professor is stopped."
main::mission_name_02_09_TheLastRide: The Last Ride of Sir Wallace
main::mission_brief_02_09_TheLastRide: You learn that one Sir Alfred Wallace is a business associate of the Professor. Perhaps he can shed light on the latter's plans and ambitions. You just received an update from a plucky street urchin informing you that Sir Wallace is heading for Fenchurch St. Station, from which you have to assume he's planning to scarper.
main::mission_debrief_02_09_TheLastRide: Sir Wallace is relieved to be on his way to Clerkenwell and beyond the Professor's clutches. As the train leaves the station, you find yourself pondering the Professor's true designs against the heart of the Empire.
main::mission_brief_02_10_FollowTheWires: You receive a report from an engineer affiliated with the Syndicate: "There's been a concerning spike in electrical output in North London. Follow the copper conduit I've indicated on the map, and discover the cause of this dangerous anomaly."
main::mission_debrief_02_10_FollowTheWir: Having dispatched the generator's unholy guardians, you end the machine's life in a similar fashion—by bashing it into pieces. When you're finished, the lights all flicker and dim to darkness. Whatever scheme the Professor is concocting, it won't be powered from here.
main::mission_objective_01_08_StrangeFru: Uncover the Source of the Unnatural Flora
main::mission_brief_01_08_StrangeFruit: Unidentified floral growths have seemingly sprouted up overnight near Rupert's Hollow, the site of an old logging clearcut. The ragged stumps and rotted logs are now riddled with the shimmering pods.
main::mission_debrief_01_08_StrangeFruit: Letting out a scream that eerily echoes the kind made by humans, the massively oversized plant crumbles to the ground. Its vines shrivel up and exude toxic fumes that fog the nighttime they shall no longer haunt. A small comfort gained in a world that remains awash with terrors.
main::mission_brief_01_10_BloodlightVigi: The wealthy landowners of Thornwood are hosting an exclusive "Moon Party." Rumours of unnatural rites and inhuman guests abound. Infiltrate and uncover their motives.
main::mission_debrief_01_10_BloodlightVi: The motives for the moon party were merely desperate half-thoughts, conjured by the mind-sickening moon. You avoided slipping into madness along with the party guests.
main::mission_brief_01_11_Changeling: A family in Epping Wake believes their daughter has been replaced by a changeling. They say the young girl in their house is in fact one of the fae, her laughter uncanny, her gaze still and unnerving. She speaks no more, but only titters madly. You offer to search for their child in the woods.
main::mission_debrief_01_11_Changeling: With the changeling banished, the family is happy to be reunited. The girl flashes an odd smile and waves goodbye as you take your leave.
main::spot_option_tavern_journal_descrip: Lose [0] Nerve, remove a card from your deck
main::spot_option_chapel_penitence_descr: Reduce Doom by [0] at the cost of [1] Vigour
main::spot_option_manor_read_description: Lose [0] Nerve, gain a rare card
main::spot_option_anselina_argue_descrip: Replace all your Tarot cards for [1] <sprite name="money">
main::spot_card_text_abandoned_farm: Visit to scavenge materials for completing your mission.
main::spot_desc_abandoned_farm: This barn has faded to a uniform grey, the doors askew, the roof collapsed. The structure hasn't been used in quite some time. An unseen shutter bangs against a jamb.
main::hub_characterselect_mollydescripti: For this agent, the nighttime streets of London are home, and the weird abominations of the Hunter's Moon nothing more than a new quarry. Her motives for joining the Syndicate are questionable, but her shotgun, blade, and trusty hound Hercules more than compensate for any misgivings her allies might harbour.
main::hub_characterselect_jerichodescrip: Jericho is a former American sailor, sapper and cook—thus his expertise with the cleaver. Though of a fearsome size, this minotaur is quiet and gentle by nature. But when provoked, he is every bit as formidable as the freakish creatures cavorting beneath the Hunter's Moon.
main::hub_characterselect_nandidescripti: Versed in the arts of alchemy, Nandi is no stranger to unnatural entities. Though still young and somewhat untested, her steady gaze speaks of hard experience, belying her years. Nandi calls upon a wide range of tactics, including hexes and animal allies, to navigate the perils of the Hunter's Moon.
main::hub_characterselect_grizwalddescri: A decorated Syndicate agent and accomplished inventor, Grizwald pilots a suit of steam-powered armour to augment his small stature. Accompanied by his automaton assistant HUGO, this gnome's gadgets and traps—as well as more mundane tools like the shotgun and hammer—are ready to put paid to the terrors of the Hunter's Moon.
main::demo_thanks_for_playing: Thanks for playing the demo! Don't forget to add us to your wishlist, and stay tuned for release!
main::hub_characterselect_emptydescripti: Repair the Airship modules to unlock new agents.
main::encounter_INS_11_description: You come across a travelling fiddler, camping by the roadside. He takes notice as you pass, but continues his song. It whispers of a terrible gang of highwaymen who terrorized the forest long ago. One of the leaders, Old Tom, was a murderous sort and was hanged at the top of the hill.
main::encounter_INS_11_option_listen_res: The melody continues, as you learn about his dying words: "I am the Devil's son, and when I return, I'll bring this land to ruin with naught but a whisper."
main::encounter_INS_11_option_ask_result: He stops to explain how the song was passed on by his grandfather, but it's not a ghost story—it's a curse.
main::encounter_INS_12_description: The lichen is spreading widely here. You observe it pulsing faintly, like ripples in a pond.
main::encounter_INS_12_option_inspect_re: The ripples have a rhythm, all leading northeast towards Hangman's Hill.
main::encounter_INS_12_option_leave_resu: The wind blows, carrying a cloud of fine dust off the whispering lichen. You cover your mouth to avoid breathing it in. Curiously, you notice that it appears to glow brightly when exposed to the moonlight.
main::encounter_INS_13_description: You pass through a hamlet, barely a few houses at the roadside, and overhear a conversation within. "We need to stay inside, ma, our neighbours are losing their damned minds."  The man sounds nervous as he consoles his mother. "The moon will pass soon, and when it does, all will be right, I promise."
main::encounter_INS_13_option_listen_res: A chill wind passes you by as you stop to eavesdrop. The man, now concerned, says, "Ma? How long have you had this rash?" You continue on.
main::encounter_INS_13_option_leave_resu: The man is right, you think, but this night won't end by itself. Something or someone is behind this.
main::status_doom_description: The power of the Chaos Moon strengthens your enemies. Their attack damage and Max <keyword_health> is increased by [0]%.
main::UI_FEEDBACK_DISCLAIMER: Basic system information and a screenshot will be sent with your report.
main::insight_progress_objective_ch1: Find More Clues About Old Tom
main::demo_end_text: Thank you for playing! We hope you enjoyed the experience. Please use the form to send us any thoughts you have about the game so far: card balance, item balance, enemy/boss difficulty, or anything else! Your feedback is invaluable to us. Thank you!
main::gameover_abandon: Wounded and weary, you flee to the Starling. You resolve to continue with renewed vigour.
main::gameover_vigour: Wounded and weary, you flee to the Starling. You resolve to continue with renewed vigour.
main::gameover_nerve: The horrors you've witnessed are too much for the mind to bear. You hasten back to the Starling, before madness consumes you.
main::gameover_insight: You've made good progress, but you're left searching for answers. You return to the Starling to resupply.
main::doom_tooltip_text: As you play travel cards to get closer to your objective, a sense of impending Doom grows, increasing the difficulty of enemy encounters.
main::mission_brief_03_01_Slavenko: Known anarchist and werewolf leader Slavenko has been running amok and inciting his comrades along with him. The fellow was already dangerous, but now the chaos moon has seemingly driven him to insanity. He must be stopped.
main::mission_debrief_03_01_Slavenko: Slavenko is dead, and with him his dastardly plot against the Empire. His fellow werewolves and co-conspirators, however, still flock together in the shadows, their grudge against Queen and Country only strengthened by this defeat.
main::mission_brief_03_02_RatKing: Something gathers in the deep and reeking tunnels between London's sewers and the Thames. The rats have taken to mobbing workers and toshers alike, almost as if organising themselves to drive Man from their domain. Find out what's behind this bizarre behaviour and put an end to it.
main::mission_debrief_03_02_RatKing: With the rat king defeated, his rodents and human followers begin to tear each other apart. You hasten away from the rending and shrieking of the doomed, and hope there's enough water in the Thames to wash them all away.
main::mission_brief_03_03_DrainHorror: A lady of high repute has complained of a creature peeping up at her from a sewer grate in Whitehall, and in her attempt to describe it, fainted away on the spot. An eldritch horror has slipped into our world from some black beyond and made its lair in the sewers beneath the city. Drive it hence, or destroy it.
main::mission_debrief_03_03_DrainHorror: That monstrosity was different from anything you've seen before, an entity not of this world and seemingly unbound by the laws that govern it. Unspeakable cosmic horrors have arrived in London, and they could overtake the Empire if they aren't stopped.
main::mission_brief_03_04_Dockhounds: The Dockhounds have grown restless as of late, and their petty crimes and harassment have burgeoned into assaults on innocent citizens. It's time someone brought these dogs to heel.
main::mission_debrief_03_04_Dockhounds: Dealing harshly with a few of their number, you've succeeded at pacifying the rest. The bloodthirsty curs have been silenced, at least for now.
main::mission_brief_03_05_GutterRats: If a group of rats is called a 'mischief,' then this is a disaster. The vermin are banding together by the dozen, and not content with their usual haunts, have been spilling out into the streets. The safety—to say nothing of the patience—of ordinary citizens is at risk.
main::mission_debrief_03_05_GutterRats: Now that their numbers have been sufficiently culled, the remaining rodents return to their filthy habits. London shall never be entirely free of them, but the problem has been throttled for the time being.
main::mission_brief_03_06_Feral: The moon's endless rays make you shiver to the bone. Or else it's the maddening howls redounding from the cobble and brick on all sides. The full moon has always been a trying time for the werewolf community, but the chaos moon seems to have driven the majority beyond reason.
main::mission_debrief_03_06_Feral: It was hard and bloody work, but at last the whines and barks of the ferocious lycanthropes are quieter for the moment. A few good citizens were bold enough to step out of their homes and thank you for your efforts.
main::mission_brief_03_07_MissingBoy: Police report that an orphan from the Sacred Heart Orphanage has gone missing. You question the Sister Superior, who tells you, "He has a fond notion of becoming an infamous highwayman. But he's a good lad. Lord as my witness, he'll get the switch this time."
main::mission_debrief_03_07_MissingBoy: You return the boy to the Sisters at the orphanage, who, naturally, chastise him the moment they're reunited. The boy is well rattled, and rightfully so. You can only hope he will stick to the orphanage grounds in the future, or at least carry a weapon next time.
main::mission_objective_03_08_SirenSong: Investigate the Show at the Palladium
main::mission_brief_03_08_SirenSong: A singer at the Palladium is said to be so alluring that some men have never returned from her performance. Apparently, many have disappeared entirely. Head to the Palladium and see what you can uncover.
main::mission_debrief_03_08_SirenSong: Now that you've vanquished the show's organisers, nobody's going to get lulled to sleep and become fodder for the Professor's experiments.
main::mission_brief_03_09_Gamble: Bare-knuckle brawls are happening at the Crimson Herring Tavern. Under the chaos moon, the sporting gents have been rather loose with their coin. If the fights cannot be stopped, then at least one with a wagering mind could take advantage of the collective madness in the East End.
main::mission_debrief_03_09_Gamble: A nice diversion from the rest of your pursuits. It's fun to play the odds, and at least for once, you weren't the one doing the fighting.
main::mission_objective_03_10_MissingRat: Return the Missing Rats to Pavel
main::mission_brief_03_10_MissingRats: A package arrives with a message from Pavel Gerard, ratcatcher and seller of fancy rats to the landed gentry. "Some of my best friends have gone missing. I'm worried they've fallen in with a bad crowd, and I need your help. By friends I mean rats, of course." Inside the package is a special kind of bait.
main::mission_debrief_03_10_MissingRats: It's always good to see Pavel. He seems to be an old friend of everyone in the East End. Glad to see his brood of rats in good health, as well. Pavel must have many ways to keep them from mutating into the gutter beasts you've seen in the sewers.
main::mission_brief_04_01_CardinalWynne: The swirling trail of Aetheric energy hangs over Trafalgar Square. Follow it to its source and put an end to the ritual.
main::mission_debrief_04_01_CardinalWynn: The swirling mouth of chaos hanging above roils and collapses in upon itself, sucking the Eclipse Cardinal into its vortex. When the haze finally dissipates, bright blue sky is revealed for the first time in what feels like weeks. The sun's rays fall gaily upon the square's ruined columns and slabs of brick, and you take a moment to revel in the warmth.
main::mission_brief_04_02_Exarch: Cultists and creatures from beyond the veil roam the streets of Westminster, working for a greater power. Pacify these minions before dealing with whoever is helming the terrible ritual that threatens London as a whole.
main::mission_debrief_04_02_Exarch: The Exarch must've been held in high regard by the cultists, but his demise doesn't halt the ritual or its effects upon the city. You gaze past the spires of Westminster Abbey towards the ultimate source of chaos, determined to make your way there as soon as you are well prepared.
main::mission_brief_04_03_Mawtouched: An abominable creature known as the Mawtouched is stalking Westminster. It cannot be left unchecked. Kill it before you carry on.
main::mission_debrief_04_03_Mawtouched: The bizarre monster wheezes one final, ragged breath and topples to the ground. As a pale mist rises from the corpse, you see that its body once held a human soul. After a brief moment of calm, a scream cuts through the quiet, reminding you that your work is not quite finished.
main::encounter_02_01A_description: The infamous Professor looks more like a vagabond than a man of science. "Too late! Too late!" he crows, skipping from one unidentifiable apparatus to another. "You're too late to stop me now. It was the glow of the chaos moon. You see, it changes everything it touches. Once it fell upon my children, they all came to life!" If reason ever graced his rolling eyes, it's long gone now.
main::encounter_02_01A_option_ItsOver_re: "You say it's over? It's only begun!" he titters madly. "You may kill me, but my children will live on, and as the power of the Chaos Moon burgeons, so will they grow." He throws a glass canister, and it shatters against your raised arm. There's no reasoning with him, you must fight him to the death.
main::encounter_02_01A_option_SmashTanks: Smash One of the Glass Tanks

The madman shrieks in horror as one of his 'children' flops onto the floor with a wet slap. It does seem to harbour a kind of life, spasming briefly before it expires in a pool of chemicals. The Professor rushes forward, slipping in the slime and cracking his head. The man's gone barking mad now, you must fight him to the death.
main::encounter_02_01A_option_BurnLabora: You set fire to every piece of equipment, ensuring that win or lose, Vayne won't be able to carry out his business here. Beakers of chemicals begin to explode as you prepare to fight.
main::encounter_02_01B_description: The makeshift conduit snaking through the city led to this macabre scene. You were hoping to find Vayne here, but the lab is abandoned. A body lies in state, surrounded by medical equipment and less identifiable machinery, his torso riddled with corroded wires. While the madman has found some success in creating new horrors, no amount of electricity will bring this boy back from the dead.
main::encounter_02_01B_option_UnplugTheM: As you unplug the machines, you notice some notes on the patient: "My son, Marsden Vayne, age twenty-one, dead from intoxication." The Professor was attempting to revive his own son, and abandoned the project. At least with the machines off, the son's departed soul will have some comfort.
main::encounter_02_01B_option_ActivateMa: The machines come to life and fill the room with strobing white light. Suddenly, sparks fly as the equipment is overloaded. The body spasms as it surges with electricity, and then bursts into flames. The fire spreads quickly, forcing you to retreat.
main::encounter_02_02A_description:
Your quarry has sequestered itself in the slums, hoping for an abandoned house or to lose itself in the blind lanes. You're deciding your next move when a raucous laugh grabs your attention, and you find yourself confronted by a pair of common thugs. Less commonly, they appear to be in league with some kind of mutant rats.
"Oi, guvnor, we've come to lighten your burden. By that we mean yer coin."
main::encounter_02_02A_option_PayThemOff: You cannot waste time skirmishing with riff-raff. You count out a generous 'fee' for their protection and hasten on your way.
main::encounter_02_02A_option_RaiseWeapo: You stride directly toward them. They spit a few threats but begin retreating backwards, tripping on the cobbles, and finally they're in full rout. They don't have the balls for a fight after all. Their hideous pets, however, are not so easily shaken.
main::encounter_02_02A_option_Fight Them: You'll not back down to filchers such as these. For Queen and Country!
main::encounter_02_02B_description: You've finally discovered your target in the next chamber ahead, not that it's making any effort at concealment, grunting and clattering about the ruins of an Arsenal warehouse. Did the Professor send his minions to collect supplies here? Every kind of gewgaw and tool is spilling from ruptured crates and fallen shelves. There might even be something you could use against the beast.
main::encounter_02_02B_option_SearchTheR: Tiptoeing through the debris, you find something of interest. But before you can collect yourself, a crate topples to the ground with a crash, alerting the creature.
main::encounter_02_02B_option_Ambush_res: You charge the Abomination with an old-fashioned battle cry!
main::encounter_02_03A_description: Reaching the Howling Twins, you're shocked to see that they don't attack you first. Instead, they contort their wolven maws to communicate. "Look at this groveling, soot-happy dollymop. The excesses of industry will never silence us. Without proud leaders like Slavenko, the populace will remain without the means to capital, wage slaves for the factories and labor camps the government calls 'offices.'"
main::encounter_02_03A_option_Argue with: You respond, arguing that capitalism is at least democratic, while anarchy and revolution are doomed to fail. The Twins howl in disagreement as they abandon any pretext of civility.
main::encounter_02_03A_option_Attack_res: Instead of wasting words on these corrupted beasts, you draw your weapon and move in for the kill. The Twins howl in tandem and prepare to defend themselves.
main::encounter_02_03B_description: You are welcomed into an austere manor by a man named Alphonse. "Thank you for coming. I lead The Silver Coin, a werewolf preservation society. The Howling Twins are besmirching the name of decorous werewolves. We cannot coexist with the citizens of London when so many of our kind are attacking recklessly. You must stop the Howling Twins so that societies like mine can continue our work."
main::encounter_02_03B_option_AgreeToHel: You point out that werewolves, when properly self-regulated, deserve the same rights as any species. You will attempt to restore honour to the Silver Coin by eliminating the Howling Twins and any other vukasin you come across who cannot control their violent urges. Alphonse's eyes brighten. "Thank you, citizen. Your efforts shall not go unnoticed. In fact, please accept this payment in advance."
main::encounter_02_03B_option_DemandPaym: You insist that your services be rewarded with capital. Alphonse silently considers this. Finally, he says, "All right. I should hope this is enough."
main::encounter_02_03B_option_Disagree o: You argue that werewolves are dangerous and unpredictable. Claiming that they will never mesh with civilized society, you say you will stop the Howling Twins, but not for Alphonse's benefit. There's a flash of silver in Alphonse's eyes before he says, "Very well, then. I will seek supporters elsewhere." You take your leave, never to return.
main::encounter_02_03C_description: A group of workers have gathered to support the Red Pack. They're chanting, "Free the wage slaves!" and hoisting painted signs. However, some are simply cursing in a disorganized manner, and as their voices rise, you see that their canines are longer than they should be, glistening in the moonlight. Something tells you this protest may indeed become violent.
main::encounter_02_03C_option_JoinThePro: You join the chanting, and a fellow hands you a sign to raise as you hit the cobbles, walking along the factory row. Bystanders and factory bosses bellow at you. "Go home, you drunken fools!" But you are determined to make your voices heard. Eventually, you slip away from the march and continue on your path.
main::encounter_02_03C_option_ChallengeP: You curse at them for opposing the status quo. Supporting the anarchist Red Pack cannot be allowed. Suddenly, a group of workers surrounds you. Baring their sharp teeth, the sound of guttural growling overpowers that of the protest. Their clothing falls to rags as they abandon their human forms and make ready to tear you limb from limb.
main::encounter_02_03C_option_Leave (Avo: It isn't worth the risk. You duck down an alley before anything turns violent.
main::encounter_02_04A_description: Here is the chapel in question, a dead body in a tradesman's apron fallen across the open threshold. As you approach, a deep, low groan sounds from within. A portly fellow with a sprung collar is watching from a safe distance, sweating profusely. You could peek through the doorway and take measure of your adversary, or else talk to this gentleman first.
main::encounter_02_04A_option_ObserveThe: You suppress a shudder as your eyes fall upon the lumbering, unnatural thing in the chapel. It appears to have been assembled from a pile of mismatched body parts, much like how Mary Shelley described her famous monster. But this is no fiction. You observe its movements for a minute, then steel yourself.
main::encounter_02_04A_option_QuestionWi: The man's staring eyes remain fixed on the doorway, even when you stand directly before him. When you speak, he jumps and hollers, "The Devil sent that creature to claim the wicked! It's the end for us all, it's the Book of Revelations, I tell you! I must find my wife..." And he scampers off. A resounding clap of wood on stone draws you running to the chapel.
main::encounter_02_05A_description: Several burly workers face off with a set of men dressed as undertakers. A cart of bodies is parked between them. "These are fallen brothers of the Red Pack. We reserve the right to bury them properly," says one of the workers. "You'll have to kill us, then! They belong to the Professor," says one of the undertakers.
main::encounter_02_05A_option_SideWithWo: You take the side of the workers, readying yourself to fight off the undertakers.
main::encounter_02_05A_option_SideWithUn: You step to the side of the undertakers, making ready to fight off the band of workers. Suddenly, they grow enraged, and transform into werewolves.
main::encounter_02_06A_description: A man approaches from the shadows, holding a white kerchief in a gesture of peace. "Hail, stranger. I see that you, too, are opposed to the vicious werewolves. Were it not for the Red Pack, my job as a monster hunter would consist of more variety. Not only that, but unless this eerie moon ever leaves us, I reckon I'll be specialising in vukasin."
main::encounter_02_06A_option_AskAboutRe: "Anarchist swine of the lupine variety," he says. "Yes, the industrialists are sick with power, but the Red Pack's spate of violence is not a way to induce a political shift. If I were Slavenko, I would raise funds and try to influence parliament. But of course, as an anarchist, he would never do that. Something tells me that someday, the Red Pack will have a more diplomatic leader."
main::encounter_02_06A_option_AskForAdvi: "Ah, but you already have your wits about you," he says. "You know quite well how to deal with our common enemy. However, as it happens, I ended up with something that is too heavy for me to carry. You might find it useful."
main::encounter_02_06A_option_Leave_resu: You leave before the white kerchief becomes a false flag and you end up in a skirmish.
main::encounter_02_07A_description: Beneath an undulating mass of rats, you see a half-eaten corpse in the street. The vermin scatter at your approach.
main::encounter_02_07A_option_ExamineBod: The rats had their fill, but there are gaping bites in his torso that could only have come from a much larger beast. You find the dead man's coin purse inside his vest pocket. Money he won't be needing anymore. As you fish it out, you hear a howl in the distance that forces you to hasten on.
main::encounter_02_07A_option_FollowRats: The rats are quick, and you give chase into an alley. All at once, they stop, turn tail, and flee past you. But from what? With a growl and a flash of teeth, a large, shadowed form rushes into the light.
main::encounter_02_08A_description: You and your companion come across a chess board in the park, with the pieces on their starting squares. You decide to play a match as a respite from the difficulties of the night. As the opening turns into the middlegame, your companion begins to ruminate.
main::encounter_02_08A_option_DiscussMen: Watching you capture a pawn with your bishop, he begins to speak. "I know that I am not the same sort of flesh and blood as you. My cells are decaying, and I can feel my bones rotting from the inside. And yet, I am still able to reason with those that would give me the extra time to sort my muddled thoughts. Will I ever recover some semblance of consciousness, enough to be your equal?" With that, he captures your queen and threatens checkmate-in-two.
main::encounter_02_08A_option_DiscussFat: "I cannot hope to live longer than a few months. My organs, in their revivified state, will eventually fail, and I will expire. I cannot know how this will all turn out for London. But if I can, I hope to see the countryside once again. I cannot stay much longer in this cesspool of violence and spiritual decay." With that, you capture his kingside bishop with your knight, opening a file for your rook to set up checkmate.
main::encounter_02_09A_description: You notice Sir Wallace at the very end of the street in front of the station. He's nervously tapping his feet and trying to seem inconspicuous. You approach from the other side and trap his escape.
main::encounter_02_09A_option_DemandInfo: After a threatening line of questioning, you get him to talk. "I ensured the Professor was well furnished for his experiments, but the equipment was supplied by someone with far more resources than I. We only communicated by telegram, but they referred to their organization as ECLIPSE. Cross my heart, that's everything I know. I only wish to cut ties and retreat to the countryside where I won't be in danger."
main::encounter_02_09A_option_TellHim_re: After you share the rumours, he tries to defend himself. "But, I never meant to associate with such a maniacal character as Professor Vayne! I only meant to contribute in some way to the advancements of science. I ensured the Professor was well furnished for his experiments, but there was another party supplying him with far more resources, and capital, to boot. We only communicated by telegram, but they referred to their organization as ECLIPSE. Cross my heart, that's everything I know! I wish I would've never been involved."
main::encounter_02_10A_description: You follow the wires to the floor of a cavernous factory. Piles of bones cover up the copper conduits, leading to an office where business was orchestrated. Crows fly in through the shattered windows, tearing into hatcheted bodies at your feet, while the rats feast on entrails scattered across the work belts.
main::encounter_02_10A_option_SearchTheO: A massive machine with sparking electric coils is bolted to the back wall, and a scribbled note sits atop the desk. "The Professor shall have all the components he needs! And all we had to do was funnel the poorhouse dead to this abandoned factory. Chop, chop! Job's a good'un. But I don't understand how the generator works." Suddenly, the smell of burning oil fills the air, and you're surrounded by mechanised enemies.
main::encounter_02_10A_option_Investigat: Following the sound of arcing electricity, you discover a massive generator in the office. A metal chute is bolted to the top, spattered with gore and what appears to be bone dust. Beside the chute on the shelf is something that is still useful. Before you can ponder the workings of the machine, you hear the sound of clanking footsteps and are surrounded by mechanised enemies.
main::encounter_02_10A_option_Leave_resu: Whatever sort of charnel house this is, at least it isn't active anymore. You hurry off before it drives you mad.
main::encounter_03_01A_description: The anarchist lycanthrope leaves a swath of shattered windows, kicked-in doors, and general mayhem in his wake, including a few hapless citizens not quick enough to flee from his path. Eventually, you track him to the docks. There! Amidst the creaking of rope and clinking of chain, Slavenko and one of his furry comrades seem to be arguing, the latter having soured on their enterprise.
main::encounter_03_01A_option_ReasonWith: Before you can do more than address him, Slavenko leaps up and pivots in the air to land before you with a snort of carrion-stinking breath. Though high overhead, the moon's reflection glows out from his eyes. Whatever reason this beast possessed has long since fled.
main::encounter_03_01A_option_PutHimDown: Before he can unleash his rage on you, you rush the beast. He turns and stumbles, taken unawares, while his friend chooses the better part of valour and gallops away into the night. You take heart; surely this fellow can't be as deadly as his reputation.
main::encounter_03_02A_description: Descending a stair below the street, you're soon navigating a maze of reeking tunnels and culverts, always seeking the highest concentration of vermin. These rodents are bold indeed, but not so bold as to challenge a flaming torch. As you approach the entrance to some kind of hall, their squeaking is hushed. A quick glance around the corner reveals a writhing heap of rats bundled into a tattered trench coat, holding court over a handful of cloaked figures and what seems like a thousand rats.
main::encounter_03_02A_option_Observe_re: This king of rats is communicating with one of its servants, a robed figure with the tail of a rat who waxes stridently about a bright future where rats will be accepted as the equal of human beings, just as the cyclopes and minotaurs of England have agitated for their rights. During an auspicious pause in this harangue, you step forward to remind the human cultists that they already enjoy these same rights. About half of them head for the door, and the Rat King cries out in violent frustration.
main::encounter_03_02A_option_BribeCulti: Stepping forward, you declare that you've news from the Queen herself: she will not recognise the Rat King's claims on the surface, but will offer a stipend to his human servants who search for new employment. Then you cast the coins upon the grime-caked floor. The cultists scramble for the money, stomping on their erstwhile allies and descending into an all-out brawl with each other. The Rat King screams in frustration and locks you with a murderous gaze from a thousand sets of eyes.
main::encounter_03_03A_description: From the offending sewer grate, you descend beneath the street via a slime-slick ladder. An irregular droning calls you to a broad confluence chamber with four inflows, all currently blocked with straw and rags. Four black-robed figures kneel in a wide circle. They're the ones droning; they otherwise appear to be insensate. An odd tingling on your scalp draws your attention upward, to where an enormous entity lies coiled against the ceiling. The idea that it might come into the light is profoundly disturbing.
main::encounter_03_03A_option_FloodTheCh: You wrestle the refuse out of the inflows, and East London's effluent begins flowing again. The cultists are completely unbothered by the dirty water sloshing around their knees, and you realise it will take hours to rise up to the thing on the ceiling. You look up again to find the unspeakable horror has begun to glow, thus revealing its true, unspeakable nature. It fixes its gaze upon you, and you find yourself shrieking in terror.
main::encounter_03_03A_option_InterruptC: You walk around to the four kneeling figures and give each of them a hard shove. They blink and stutter and fall to rolling and crawling in the muck, disoriented to the point of helplessness. But their droning has stopped. With a sound like giant soap bubbles popping, the squalid growth above you shrieks and uncoils. As it descends, you notice that one of the fools has dropped something interesting.
main::encounter_03_04A_description: You come across a dwarf acquaintance by the name of Skinner. He's a deadbeat and a layabout, but not a criminal as far as you're aware. He's drawing for high card in the street with a couple of other cadgers, and invites you to join. You need capital to play.
main::encounter_03_04A_option_Play_resul: You play a few rounds, but the Fates don't smile upon you. When the others have wandered off, Skinner holds three different Kings aloft, and you see that they're distinctly warped. "Always check your tools first, cully." He tips his hat and saunters off with a jaunty step and a jingle in his pocket.
main::encounter_03_04A_option_Watch_resu: You stay awhile and watch Skinner draw high card after high card until he cleans out the others. Your instincts tell you he's cheating, but you can't see how he does it. You do feel a bit rested, though.
main::encounter_03_05A_description: You take a break from scourging rats to watch a young girl tossing a brightly-coloured ball in the air, until it slips from her grasp and rolls down a dark, perfectly round hole of the exact diameter to swallow it. She begins to wail.
main::encounter_03_05A_option_ReachIntoH: You reach cautiously into the hole and grope around. It's oddly deep, and you find you're sunk in to the shoulder and still haven't found the ball. As you're withdrawing your arm, something seizes you by the wrist and yanks. You wrestle yourself free and find something, but not a ball, gripped in your hand.
main::encounter_03_05A_option_UseStick_r: You prod about for a bit but feel nothing like a soft ball, until some unseen force pulls on the other end of the wood and nearly jerks you down in the dirt. The girl laughs delightedly when the stick vanishes into the hole. At least she's forgotten about her missing ball.
main::encounter_03_05A_option_Leave_resu: You haven't the time to do any small favours. The city needs you.
main::encounter_03_06A_description: Seeing candlelight in the window of a chapel, you enter to sit and rest for a few minutes. A robed figure approaches from behind—the pastor must have been out on an errand—and rests his hand on your shoulder. You turn. A grievous wound at some point opened his cheek on one side, which explains the wicked leer on his face. "Tell me," he says, "what do you think of polytheism, that is, the belief in a host of different gods?"
main::encounter_03_06A_option_ReferenceT: You reply that Christianity's holy trinity could be considered three different divinities. For some reason, this answer infuriates him to sputtering incoherence. Although you make to leave, his hard hand pins you in place as his obscene grin stretches until it rips open his old scar. Finally, you duck down, slip from his grasp, and make a hasty exit.
main::encounter_03_06A_option_DiscussPol: You enter into a spirited discussion that touches on Hinduism and the Ancient Greeks, later moving to much more esoteric and decidedly unholy systems of belief. When you take your leave, he presses something into your hands. You shrug and accept his gift, although it is odd coming from a priest.
main::encounter_03_07A_description: You're passing weary when you finally locate the lad. There he stands in the darkest, dirtiest corner of an alley beside the Orphanage, ankle-deep in refuse, shivering and sobbing. After a quick glance at his surroundings, you can hardly blame him.
main::encounter_03_07A_option_BarkAtHim_: Bark at Him to Come Along

"Oi!" you call out. "The good sisters want you back, stripling. Hop to it!" Beckoning the boy, you freeze in place as a cold blade alights upon your jugular. "Take a breath, guvnor. It's just yer purse I want." The robber nicks your capital, then sends you sprawling with a shove and darts away. Well, at least you found the boy.
main::encounter_03_07A_option_ApproachGe: With open arms and a friendly smile, you step towards the child—and are caught short by the clatter of a kicked bottle and a shouted curse at your back. You came within a hair's breadth of being ambushed!
main::encounter_03_08A_description: You buy a ticket and take your seat for the performance. By intermission, you see that several men have been hypnotised, and the staff begins to drag their bodies backstage. Lord knows what fate shall befall them if you don't put a stop to this eerie show.
main::encounter_03_08A_option_Argue_resu: "You there!" you call out. "I won't have you kidnapping honest men like this. They don't deserve whatever you've got planned for them back there." A group of thugs surrounds you and cajole you outside. Now you've got no choice but to best them in combat.
main::encounter_03_08A_option_AttackOrga: You grapple with one of the men dragging a body. He drops his prize, but soon enough, you're surrounded by thugs and pushed outside. Fighting them all at once is your only option now.
main::encounter_03_09A_description: You enter the tavern and have a drink. A happy pause from the mayhem outside. Budgie Babcock stands behind the betting counter. He's a well-known oddsman who at least makes good on his bets. You survey the two gents who are warming up to brawl, wondering how much you should wager.
main::encounter_03_09A_option_BetOnFavor: You bet on the well-tuned machine of a boxer in the red corner. A wall of flesh and fur that you'd swear is part minotaur.
main::encounter_03_09A_option_BetOnUnder: You bet on the lanky boxer in the blue corner, hoping that his range and speed will get the best of his opponent.
main::encounter_03_09A_option_WagerNothi: You decide not to risk it, but you pay the fee to watch the fight.
main::encounter_03_10A_description: Those rats have the markings that Pavel mentioned. See if you can lure them closer.
main::encounter_03_10A_option_GiveSpecia: The rats scurry over and join your party.
main::encounter_03_11A_description: "What ho!" says Pavel when he sees you at the meeting place. The rats scurry around in circles at his feet, then crawl inside his waistcoat and take up their familiar positions of comfort. "My beloved friends have been returned. How can I reward you?"
main::encounter_03_11A_option_AskForReli: You ask Pavel if he has anything rare to offer you. "But of course," Pavel says, handing you a bundle with a small item inside.
main::encounter_03_11A_option_AskForCard: You ask Pavel for something that you could use in battle. "But of course," Pavel says, handing you a small item folded in a silk kerchief.
main::encounter_03_11A_option_AskMoney_r: You ask to be paid in capital, and Pavel is happy to oblige.
main::encounter_04_01A_description: You step gingerly out upon the once-grand square, its statues now scarred, its pavements littered with rubble. The glowering, mad-eyed Wynne awaits. Surrounded by his followers, it's clear that the ritual is nearly complete. "I am Eclipse Cardinal Edwin Wynne. I will destroy anyone who stands in my way."
main::encounter_04_02A_description: A hooded figure with red eyes towers before you. In a crackling voice that grates on the ears, he says, "I am the Exarch, lieutenant to the Cardinal. I see that your aim is to reach Cardinal Wynne. By that same request, you have asked me to take your life."
main::encounter_04_03A_description: Here is the beast that was stalking the streets of Westminster, slaying anything foolish enough to cross its path. From the ruined features that pass for its face, you surmise that it was once human. Most likely a cultist, infected with corruption from beyond the veil.
main::encounter_INS_21_description: A weary chimney sweep questions you cautiously. "I found something while I was cleaning the stacks, and need your help."
main::encounter_INS_21_option_Investigat: The furnace smells of rot as you peer in. Through the flames, you can see the bones of a corpse. Perhaps the fire wasn't hot enough to completely incinerate the evidence.
main::encounter_INS_21_option_question_r: He walks away nervously, but not before warning you: "There are strange deeds happening in these streets. No one is safe."
main::encounter_INS_22_description: The street lamps suddenly flicker out around you, before blinking back on a moment later.
main::encounter_INS_22_option_Examine_re: These lamps must use new electric wires. Your eye follows the wires down the street and you notice more thick wiring haphazardly strung up. It leads to a large Aether refinery at the top of a hill.
main::encounter_INS_23_description: You find a discarded stack of papers, torn and blood spattered. They appear to be bulletins.
main::encounter_INS_23_option_ReadAnnoun: "Workers unite!" is scrawled broadly. "Join the Red Pack and fight for your liberty!" A red 'A' for anarchy is scribbled below.
main::encounter_INS_31_description: A street urchin flags you down and shares a message. "I knows a copper wot wants to speak to you. Life or death, I reckon. Find him at St. Katherine's, quick as you can."
main::encounter_INS_31_option_MeetStrang: A nervous constable waits near the water. "I heard about your connections with the Crown, and I don't know where else to turn. The police are all on the take, likely up to Scotland Yard. Paid informants for a man they call Cardinal Wynne. Days ago, before this moon business, we were all told to leave Westminster well enough alone. I believe something infernal is taking place there."
main::encounter_INS_31_option_IgnoreMess: Wandering under the moon's bleeding light, you spot a body washed ashore in the Thames. Clutched in his hand is a police bulletin, wet but legible: "Our friend, Cardinal Wynne, requests officers refrain from their appointed duties in the vicinity of Westminster until further notice. There is no room for non-compliance. Commissioner Warren." What could possess the police to follow this order?
main::encounter_INS_32_description: As you walk near the Thames, you see some peculiar movement near the water.
main::encounter_INS_32_option_Investigat: You walk to the end of a dock and peer into the black water. Without warning, you see a gigantic creature breach the surface. Its flesh is a collage of violets, sickly greens, and otherworldly hues, covered in countless bulging eyes. It dips back into the shallow depths, leaving you questioning your sanity.
main::encounter_INS_32_option_Watch_resu: A swirling haze rises from the river. At first it is inky black, but then it seems that a thousand stars burn behind it, as if through a two-way mirror. Hanging in thin air, you realize that it must be a window across space. A large black... thing... slips through and splashes into the river, before the window closes, and the water is still again.
main::encounter_INS_33_description: As you reflect on the strange occurrences of the past few nights, you bump into a gentleman quite by accident, causing him to drop a strange purple mask. He retrieves it and hurriedly rushes off. You follow him covertly and observe as he enters a Mason's Lodge, unaware that he is being tailed.
main::encounter_INS_33_option_EnterLodge: You walk into a grand foyer. A group of figures stands in a circle wearing masks and purple robes. You are spotted at once, and several members rush at you with blades in their hands.
main::encounter_INS_33_option_SneakInsid: Through a second story balcony you gain access to the lodge. From a safe vantage, you quickly see these are no Masons. Forming a circle, you see a dozen or more figures in purple cloaks and matching, smiling masks. One of them speaks: "Brothers, the hour is at hand. The ritual is nearly complete. The master is coming! All hail Erebus! All hail the son of Chaos!" The group cheers in unison. "Now, you all know what is necessary: nothing gets into Westminster. See to it." You leave quietly, but not before finding something of value on a bookshelf by the window.
main::mission_brief_00_01_Nandi: A missive arrives. “Nandi, I have news. Under this strange moon, my tarot cards are predicting many things, but here is a glimmer of hope in the chaos. The Fates indicate that a medicine can be made from the lichen, after it has combined with an animal's blood. Collect a few samples from the lichen-riddled animals, and we can craft a curative.” - Anselina Kemény
main::mission_debrief_00_01_Nandi: With a new curative formed from the whispering lichen, you are filled with hope that somehow, you'll be on the other side of this madness soon enough.
main::mission_brief_00_02_Jericho: A missive arrives. “Jericho, I need your help. A group of Vayne’s minions has produced a dangerous chemical weapon. The lingering moon has the bobbies scattered about, so the police won’t have time for this. The evildoers are demanding payment from the Crown, or else claiming to release the chemical weapon on our citizens. You need to stop them and neutralize the weapon.” - Rajan, Agent of the Crown
main::mission_debrief_00_02_Jericho: With the chemical weapon safely neutralised, you find yourself with a spare moment to breathe. But as you pour a drink to toast to your accomplishment, a sharp cry echoes through the night. Whether a cry for help or a warning of some kind, it appears that your work is still cut out for you.
main::mission_brief_00_03_Molly: A missive arrives. “Molly, I know it left a bad taste when I looted that wagon full of jewerly you’d stolen with the Forty Bullfrogs. I want to make things right, but first I need a favour. Bandits have been sticking up my shipments of gin, whilst half of my Dockhounds have gone mad from the moon. In order to restore some order to the gangs, I need you to put down the bandit leader, Markus Cohn.” - Jack O’Malley, leader of the Dockhounds
main::mission_debrief_00_03_Molly: This isn't the first time you've killed on contract, and the way things are going, it won't be the last. Markus Cohn is a bag of bones, and Jack has an idea for a rendezvous in the future. That could be intriguing, but for now, you've got your hands full. There are monsters about London, and people in need of help.
main::mission_brief_00_04_Grizwald: A missive arrives. “Grizwald, my latest contract for the Crown has gone to the dogs. Or should I say rats? Lady Aegis was a wondrous combat automaton, until she began to practice on my own engineers. Several were injured when we fled the secret workshop in the sewers. Now, the rat cultists have discovered my project and are trying to control her. The machine must be terminated. Grizwald, you’re the only person I know who is brilliant and brave enough to do it.” - Ada Lovelace, Enchantress of Numbers
main::mission_debrief_00_04_Grizwald: Hopefully Ada will bounce back from this. She's one of London's brightest mathematical minds. And, because chaos is breaking out with abandon, there's a good chance the Crown will need her help in the future.
main::insight_progress_objective_ch2: Search the East End for Answers
main::status_berserk_jericho_description: You deal and receive [0]% more damage.
main::status_rage_jericho_description: At [N] stacks, lose all <keyword_rage> and gain <keyword_berserk> for one turn.
main::encounter_INS_22_option_ExamineYou: A bill stuck to the wall catches your eye. "Join the Dockhounds! Fair pay and protection from Slavenko and the Red Pack!" Scrawled underneath in a red grease pen is a vandal's slogan: "Nobody is safe until they join Professor Vayne!"
main::encounter_INS_23_option_MakeSureYo: A figure with a hunchback emerges from the shadows. "Don't worry, I'm too weak to harm you, even if I tried. Those bulletins are spread by the Red Pack. They're trying to rally the local werewolves to revolt. Anarchists, some say, revolutionists, say others." The figure disappears as quickly as it came, leaving a foul odor of sweat and tobacco.
main::encounter_04_01A_option_AppealToLo: To which he replies, "There can be no logic when the void is eternal." He makes ready to fight.
main::encounter_04_01A_option_RemindCost: To which he replies, "Human life is a bargain in exchange for the great power of Erebus, the Son of Chaos. Prepare to die or yield to the all-knowing void."
main::encounter_04_02A_option_Defy_resul: Looking the Exarch dead in the eyes, you summon all the courage you can muster and say to him, "Whatever insane plot you have devised, the Syndicate will stop you."
main::encounter_04_02A_option_Prepare_re: You take a moment to check your kit and weapons before facing the inevitable horror of your seething opponent.
main::encounter_04_03A_option_Face_resul: You curse at the creature in defiance, promising to banish it back to whatever twisted hell it came from. It bellows with fury and bears its many teeth.
main::encounter_04_03A_option_Approach_r: Taking cover behind a park bench, you flank the beast and use the moment to ready yourself before the battle ahead.
main::encounter_02_02A_option_FightThem_: You'll not back down to filchers such as these. For Queen and Country!
main::encounter_02_03A_option_ArgueWithO: You respond, arguing that capitalism is at least democratic, while anarchy and revolution are doomed to fail. The Twins howl in disagreement as they abandon any pretext of civility.
main::encounter_02_03B_option_DisagreePr: You argue that werewolves are dangerous and unpredictable. Claiming that they will never mesh with civilized society, you say you will stop the Howling Twins, but not for Alphonse's benefit. There's a flash of silver in Alphonse's eyes before he says, "Very well, then. I will seek supporters elsewhere." You take your leave, never to return.
main::encounter_02_03C_option_Leave_resu: It isn't worth the risk. You duck down an alley before anything turns violent.
main::status_nandi_lion_description: <keyword_mark>. At the start of your turn, gain [N] <keyword_energy>. Only 1 <keyword_mark> can be active at a time.
main::status_nandi_hawk_description: <keyword_mark>. At the start of your turn, draw 2 cards. Only 1 <keyword_mark> can be active at a time.
main::status_nandi_impala_description: <keyword_mark>. <keyword_block> is not lost at the beginning of the turn. Only 1 <keyword_mark> can be active at a time.
main::status_nandi_snake_description: <keyword_mark>. Enemies take 50% more poison damage. Only 1 <keyword_mark> can be active at a time.
main::status_defensive_description: <keyword_block> gained from cards and abilities is increased by [X].
main::spot_hunters_moon_missionbreak_des: Restore health and gain bonuses from airship upgrades
main::spot_desc_hunters_moon_missionbrea: Take a turn at ease before continuing to the next mission
main::spot_desc_hunters_moon_start: Start your run with bonuses based on upgrades to the airship
main::spot_option_anselina_donate_descri: Give Wooden Tarot Box, receive a Rare relic
main::spot_option_chapel_offering_descri: Give Prayer Beads, reduce Doom by [0]
main::spot_option_manor_specimen_descrip: Give Specimen Jar, receive a Rare relic
main::spot_option_tavern_drinks_descript: Give 1875 Chateau Lajoie, reduce Doom by [0]
main::location_woods: In the countryside north of London, the townsfolk speak in hushed tones of something lurking in the forest. Rumours abound of the vengeful spirit of Old Tom, an infamous highwayman hanged in the woods long ago. What has people most concerned, however, is the rapid spread of a new disease folks call 'The Whisper.'
main::location_industry: In the industrial outskirts of London's East End, the deranged Professor Alaric Vayne has discovered an unholy new technology. Under the eerie glow of the unending moon, rusted machinery brings flesh to life. Meanwhile, the streets become even more chaotic, as the 'vukasin,' or werewolf contagion is carried on the wings of an anarchist revolution.
main::location_docklands: London's East Docklands are in chaos. Slavenko, the werewolf and anarchist leader of the Red Pack, expands his influence beneath the full moon. Meanwhile, in the sewers and catacombs of the Underground, strange creatures skitter and chant about a ritual that will bring about endless darkness.
main::location_westminster: The shadowy organization ECLIPSE, and their cult followers, have taken over Westminster. Horrifying creatures from beyond the veil now stalk the cobbles beneath Big Ben. Their leader, Cardinal Wynne, has nearly finished a ritual that could destroy London or worse.
main::encounter_02_Sootfall_description: The chimneystacks above belch not just smoke but a black, clinging ash. It drifts down in unnatural sheets, sticking to your coat and lashes.
main::encounter_02_Sootfall_option_WipeA: You brush the soot aside, but it smears like ink. Beneath it, faint runes flicker red before fading.
main::encounter_02_Sootfall_option_Ignor: Best not to dwell on omens. You lower your head and march through the falling ash, feeling its grit grind between your teeth.
main::encounter_02_MangledAutomaton_desc: A broken machine lies twitching against a soot-stained wall, gears still clicking faintly.
main::encounter_02_MangledAutomaton_opti: You pry open its chestplate. Inside is a glowing coil of aetheric energy, humming with artificial life. You notice it has been dislodged, and set it right. The creature bursts to life, clanking with an appreciative tone.
You dash its head with a nearby brick. The mechanisms stop, and steam sighs from it like a dying breath. You take what gears and cogs you deem valuable and step away.
main::encounter_02_HowlInTheYard_descrip: From the shadows, a ragged worker growls at you. His eyes burn like coals, and he tears at his shirt in anguish.
main::encounter_02_HowlInTheYard_option_: You raise your voice, calling on what authority you can. He snarls, then bolts off into the night, rattling the fences.
You slip away before his transformation is complete. Moments later, a chorus of howling erupts through the streets.
main::encounter_02_GrayTide_description: The ground seems to ripple, as rats pour from drains in endless numbers. Their claws scratch the stone like raindrops.
main::encounter_02_GrayTide_option_Stand: You kick at them, scattering the vanguard. The swarm parts around you with a strange discipline.
main::encounter_02_GrayTide_option_FindH: You clamber on to a nearby cart. From above, you watch the vermin stream past, like soldiers marching to war. In a moment, the street is calm once more.
main::encounter_02_ShatteredMask_descrip: A porcelain mask lies broken on the steps, one half human—smiling, the other shattered into teethlike shards.
main::encounter_02_ShatteredMask_option_: The ceramic is still warm. Whispers coil into your ear before the visage crumbles to dust in your palm.
You step carefully past the hideous thing. A vagrant sitting on the steps beside you mutters, "Don't want any part in their business. ECLIPSE is everywhere. Best left untouched."
main::encounter_02_AbandonedCart_descrip: A greengrocer's cart has been left in the street. Rotting cabbages have spilled out onto the cobblestones.
main::encounter_02_AbandonedCart_option_: Hidden under the mouldering produce, you find a few soiled books. Perhaps one among them could be useful.
You walk past the mess, but hear the faint crunch of teeth gnashing beneath it. Some filthy rats emerge, their eyes glowing in the moonlight.
main::encounter_02_StrayHound_descriptio: A massive hound sits beneath a lamppost, coat bristled, muzzle slick with something dark. Its eyes reflect silver as it regards your approach.
main::encounter_02_StrayHound_option_Pla: Its presence blocks your path. You search your pockets for something to throw, and lob it into a nearby alley. The hound yawns with disinterest and lays its head on its enormous paws. You pass with caution.
main::encounter_02_StrayHound_option_App: The hound growls, then drops something covered in slobber at your feet. He stands and plods off into the night.
main::encounter_03_CrackedStatue_descrip: In a quiet courtyard, a marble angel stands eroded and smoke-stained. Its eyes appear to be weeping from the soot. The ground around it emits a dewy mist.
main::encounter_03_CrackedStatue_option_: You spot some coins by the statue's foot. As you pick them up, the soot sticks to your finger like indelible ink.
The angel's marble head seems to turn as you pass, following your steps with its ruined gaze until you're gone.
main::encounter_03_CallToArms_descriptio: A red banner reads, "FREEDOM FOR WORKERS." It flaps limply, torn and splattered with something darker than soot.
main::encounter_03_CallToArms_option_Pul: You pull the cloth free. The stains reek of blood, but stuck to its folds you find a damp strike leaflet. The manifesto is trite, but as you read its words you feel inspired.
main::encounter_03_CallToArms_option_Lea: As you reflect on the banner's call, you hear a sound behind you. A pack of feral, wolven men glare at you from the shadows.
main::encounter_03_StrangeEnterprise_des: Near the docks, you find a rowboat stacked with barrels. Strange, glowing oil spills into the water. Some Dockhounds are playing a drinking game under lamplight not far away.
main::encounter_03_StrangeEnterprise_opt: The barrels are marked with a crescent moon, an image you've seen before. The fluid inside can only be Aether, just as valuable as it is volatile. You spot something useful nearby on the ground, and deftly swipe it before you leave.

You overhear the Dockhounds. "What’re we doin’ with this lot o' barrels?" one of them says. "The Cardinal paid handsomely for 'em," the other replies. "But it's a long haul to Westminster." They finish their drinks. "Fetch one o' the other lads to do it. I ain't going near that place."
main::encounter_03_CrackedVeil_descripti: The air above the street ripples like heat haze, though the night is bitter cold. Shapes swim in the distance, impossibly vast.
main::encounter_03_CrackedVeil_option_In: You draw closer to the shimmering veil, and find a face staring back at you. At first it looks like a mirror but its form is unclear. A peel of energy rips across the veil, and a creature both hideous and alien steps onto the street.
main::encounter_03_CrackedVeil_option_Ke: You avert your gaze. Still, you feel eyes beneath the water lock onto you, following until you leave.
main::encounter_03_ShutteredDen_descript: You pass through Red Lantern Lane, and see that a smoking den has been boarded up here, the scent of incense and decay still leaking through.
main::encounter_03_ShutteredDen_option_F: Inside, the bodies of patrons lie slack in chairs, eyes wide but pupils gone, as though something fed on their dreams.
main::encounter_03_ShutteredDen_option_P: You keep moving. From behind the boards comes the faint sound of chanting in a language not your own.
main::encounter_03_GangTrial_description: A circle of young street toughs surround a terrified old man. At first they jeer and poke with crude weapons, but are taken aback when the man lets out an unearthly howl.
main::encounter_03_GangTrial_option_Inte: You yell out at the crowd and they turn to see you. The old man, now fur clad and feral accepts the distraction and lopes into the alley at great speed. A few of the thugs brandish their weapons at you for ruining their fun.
main::encounter_03_GangTrial_option_Watc: The old man stands tall, now feral and wolfen. He charges viciously into the boys, tearing one limb from limb. The boys, no longer laughing, flee into the streets. The wolf growls as it takes notice of you.
main::encounter_03_SmilingMan_descriptio: A tall figure in a too-long coat leans against the wall, grinning with a mouth of filed teeth. "Heads or Tails?" He sneers, holding out a silver coin.
main::encounter_03_SmilingMan_option_Hea: He flips the coin. It gleams briefly in the moonlight before he catches it in his clawed hand. "Heads," he says with a hint of disappointment. "You may keep the coin. Spend it fast as you can, mind you, the Hand of Chaos will be here soon!"
main::encounter_03_SmilingMan_option_Tai: The coin tumbles through the air and he snatches it deftly. "Tails, you lose!" He laughs ominously and steps back into the shadow. In his place, you see a new shape appear, ominous and beastly.
main::encounter_03_SplinteredWharf_descr: The timbers creak, freshly broken. Something massive passed through here and dragged itself from the river on its way to the street.
main::encounter_03_SplinteredWharf_optio: You find a trail of luminous slime still steaming in the cold. Its gelatinous mass is riddled with tiny, blinking eyes.
You retreat quickly. The water slaps against the wharf with an unnatural rhythm, like a heartbeat.
main::form_command_description: Force all enemies to attack you instead of your allies
main::form_vanguard_description: Each consecutive attack on a turn deals an additional [0] Damage
main::status_windup_description: Next attack deals additional damage equal to Wind-up. Lost when hit.
main::gameover_victory: After vanquishing the evil in the area, you report back to the Starling. Take a moment to revel in your victory, but remember that the Syndicate doesn't rest. There is always work to do.
main::reward_gamble_favorite: Wager [0] <sprite name="money">, 70% chance to win [1] <sprite name="money">
main::reward_gamble_underdog: Wager [0] <sprite name="money">, 30% chance to win [1] <sprite name="money">
main::status_chaotic_description: Randomly changes intention when receiving unblocked attack damage
main::cutscene_enginefixed_a: Have you had any success with the repairs?
main::cutscene_enginefixed_b: I've just replaced the Centrifugal Governor, so the Starling should be skyworthy once more. And not a moment too soon! The moon's radiation is growing stronger.
main::cutscene_enginefixed_c: The forests are quaint, but I would welcome a change of scenery.
main::cutscene_enginefixed_d: That's an important reminder, thank you. The Starling's facilities are still damaged. Perhaps with more salvage, we can clear some space for needed allies along the way.
main::cutscene_westminster_a: Westminster has become a fortress, and the police have turned a blind eye to whatever mayhem is happening there.
main::cutscene_westminster_b: After the horrors we've encountered, I have no doubt the streets will be swarming with far worse.
main::cutscene_westminster_c: I concur. This 'Bishop' and his ECLIPSE cultists are well prepared. This will be a difficult fight.
main::cutscene_westminster_d: We have no choice. We know that their ritual concludes tonight. Whatever that means, it has to be stopped.
main::cutscene_firstjericho_a: As you know, an army is only as good as its logistics. I have invited my old friend, Jericho, to join us. He will be in charge of our supplies and manage our Armoury.
main::cutscene_firstjericho_b: You look like you'd be handy in a scrap too.
main::cutscene_firstjericho_c: I prefer peace, but if necessary, yes. But my concern is keeping us all well stocked.
main::cutscene_firstjericho_d: In either case, we're glad to have you.
main::cutscene_firstnandi_a: With our laboratory in working order again, I've invited Nandi to join our crew. She is an expert botanist, and I'm glad to share my research with her.
main::cutscene_firstnandi_b: Your encounters with this 'Whisper' are concerning, to say the least.
main::cutscene_firstnandi_c: With things the way they are, we need all the help we can get.
main::status_cowardly_description: This character will flee if it has no allies.
main::tooltip_energy: Cards cost Energy to play. When your energy is spent, end your turn. At the start of your turn, new cards are drawn and Energy is restored to maximum.
main::tooltip_vigour: <sprite name="health">Vigour represents the agent's health. If it is reduced to 0, the mission has failed.
main::tooltip_nerve: <sprite name="sanity">Nerve represents the agent's sanity or will. If it is reduced to 0, the mission has failed.
main::tooltip_coins: Earned <sprite name="money"> can be spent in encounters to purchase new cards, relics, and consumables.
main::tooltip_salvage: Find <sprite name="bricks">salvage to help Grizwald repair the Starling, unlocking new upgrades and agents.
main::tooltip_exp: Battles and encounters provide the agent valuable training experience. Aboard the Starling, experience provides <sprite name="supersphere">Talent Points for purchasing new agent upgrades.
main::tooltip_newcard: Your agent has earned a new card. Once collected, it will be added to your agent's deck.
main::tooltip_upgradecard: One of your agent's cards can be upgraded, improving its abilities.
main::spot_desc_chapeltown: An unassuming church stands near the high street. Inside, parishioners kneel in quiet prayer among the pews. You hear a choir singing in the distance, though no such group is present here.
main::spot_desc_manortown: The sharp silhouette of gothic towers against the night sky leads you to an old boys' school. Whatever students attend this place, none are present now. You decide to take shelter in the library, where a peculiar old man is reading in the antiquities section. He glances around nervously, and slips away into the maze of shelving.
main::spot_desc_taverntown: At the end of a labyrinthine tangle of dark alleys, you find a public house. A rosy-cheeked fellow stumbles out and tips his hat to you, before wandering off into the night. Through the swinging door, you see a weary barman serving the sombre patrons.
main::spot_desc_storage_shed: You find a small store room at the edge of a workers' dormitory. The door is padlocked but a window stands conveniently ajar.
main::encounter_02_08B_description: The crowd disperses, and the creature tells you he has no memory of his past, only of the man who gave him this new life: The Professor. "I need to flee the city or he'll kill me."
main::encounter_02_08B_option_OfferProte: Despite the crowd's brutality, he appears to have healed at an astonishing rate. "I can be handy in a scrap, if you'll let me," he says with optimism. If you can get him to the river, he can escape by boat, but you'll have to get past this mob first.
main::encounter_02_08B_option_LeadTheWay: His appearance is horrifying, but you see kindness in his eyes. "If you can help me get to the river, I'll leave by boat. The trains are too conspicuous." As the angry mob rallies towards you, you don't think the streets will be much safer.
main::encounter_02_08C_description: You reach the end of the street, and hear the running water of a nearby river. If he takes a boat downstream to the Thames, he may be able to leave the city safely. He looks to you with uncertainty.
main::encounter_02_08C_option_WishPeace_: You give him encouragement, hoping that he finds a quiet place to contemplate his remaining time here. You were glad for his company and learned from him in some strange way.
main::encounter_02_08C_option_SearchForA: "The Syndicate will watch your back, wherever you end up," you say, hoping to provide some comfort. "Thank you," he replies. "I'd like to learn more about the man I once was, with the days I have left. Oh, take this as thanks, I won't need it."
main::tutorial_mission_select_1_desc: With the Starling's engines repaired, new mission locations are now available.
main::tutorial_mission_select_2_desc: The ECLIPSE ritual at Westminster must be stopped. A new mission location is now available.
main::tutorial_repair_menu_desc: The Starling's facilities were damaged. Spend salvage to refurbish them and unlock new capabilities and agents to support your missions.
main::tutorial_talent_menu_desc: XP earned from missions will increase the level of your agents. Each level increase provides all unlocked agents with a Talent point towards the purchase of unique agent abilities.
main::warning_unpicked_items_description: Any unclaimed Tarot or Consumable rewards will be left behind.
main::credits_based_on: Based on Sovereign Syndicate, Created by
main::tutorial_page5_note1_text: Play Travel Cards by placing them on the path. Travel Cards come in various types: battles, narrative encounters, and locations. Travel Cards are shuffled into one or more decks; hover over a deck to peek ahead.
main::tutorial_page6_note1_text: Your mission objective and progress are shown on the screen. Mission objectives and other clues are shuffled into the travel decks or placed on the path. If your Vigour or Nerve reach zero, you fail the mission.
main::tutorial_page7_note1_text: Travel Cards cost <sprite name="fuel">Doom to play. At set intervals, the Doom level will increase and strengthen your enemies. Doom can also be affected by consumables, relics, tarot cards, and encounters.
main::hub_exp_congrats_sign: Congratulations! From now on, you will come across these cards on your next travels!
main::hub_exp_congrats_items_sign: Congratulations! From now on, you will come across these items on your next travels!
main::hub_exp_congrats_fail_text: This character no longer has additional cards, in order to continue to receive rewards and fun from the game - try play for other characters.
main::hub_demo_over_title: Congratulations! You have successfully completed the demo version.
main::hub_demo_over_text:
The storyline of the Menace from the Deep demo version has concluded. You can continue playing to unlock additional locked content available in the demo.

If you enjoyed the game, be sure to add the game to your Wishlist, as this directly influences whether the full version of the game will be released.

Thank you for playing. Don't be afraid of anything, and recommend the demo version to your friends.
