main::starting_deck_detective_a_descript: A basic deck with mostly attacking cards and tricks for an aggressive game.
main::starting_deck_detective_b_descript: A basic deck with mostly defensive cards and tricks for a moderate and safe start.
main::starting_deck_detective_c_descript: A basic deck with exclusively dirty tricks. Suitable for players who are already familiar with the game.
main::starting_deck_detective_d_descript: A basic deck with close combat moves. Your own body is the best weapon if mastered flawlessly.
main::starting_deck_detective_e_descript: A basic deck focused on quickly accumulating rage.
main::starting_deck_detective_f_descript: A basic deck designed for playing with allies. Give orders and cover team members from danger.
main::starting_deck_professor_a_descript: A basic deck focuses on laboratory rats and experiments on them.
main::starting_deck_professor_b_descript: A basic deck with mostly poisonous attack cards.
main::starting_deck_professor_c_descript: A basic deck focused on the professor's mad form. Aggressive in gameplay but also risky for the player.
main::starting_deck_professor_d_descript: A basic deck focused on manipulating sanity, containing powerful cards that drain sanity but also allow its recovery.
main::starting_deck_professor_e_descript: A basic deck that includes attack cards with both fire and cold effects.
main::starting_deck_professor_f_descript: A basic deck focused on laboratory birds and experiments on them.
main::starting_deck_cultist_a_descriptio: A basic deck focused on inflicting bleeding and quickly gaining bloodlust.
main::starting_deck_cultist_b_descriptio: A basic deck focused on various sneaky tricks.
main::starting_deck_cultist_c_descriptio: A basic deck with a predominance of cards for managing cards in hand.
main::starting_deck_cultist_d_descriptio: A basic deck with a predominance of cards focused on achieving high attack rates.
main::starting_deck_cultist_e_descriptio: A basic deck with balanced offensive and defensive potential and without specific cultist mechanics.
main::starting_deck_cultist_f_descriptio: A basic deck with a powerful combination of cards, but will it be useful in every battle?
main::details_panel_relic_block_descript: Build [Building] of level [Level] to receive an additional relic at the start
main::cards_wiki_menu_description: Here you can view all available cards. Some cards need to be unlocked by increasing the level of fears consumed by the monolith at the end of each journey. Hovering over a card reveals details of some of its actions. Clicking on an unlocked card will show all its possible upgrades.
main::items_wiki_menu_description: Here you can view all available inventory items. Some of them need to be unlocked by increasing the level of fears consumed by the monolith at the end of each journey. Hovering over an item reveals details of its properties.
main::motel_story: On your way, you come across a motel. It's surprisingly cozy for these lands. What if it's the only place around where you can catch your breath?
main::museum_story:
You have entered the museum. There's no one around, and the dead silence makes the situation even more suspicious and unclear. 

In the museum, there are numerous interesting artifacts. You decide to take a closer look to see if there are any items with dark energy here.
main::warehouse_story: Driving through the forest, you notice above the tree canopies the tall roofs of industrial buildings and smoking chimneys piercing the already overcast sky. The journey is not short, but you decide to head there.
main::gas_station_story: Driving past the gas station, you decide to stop and take a break from the long journey. The lights are on, but there is no service personnel around.
main::asylum_story: This place exudes gloom. The mad cries from inside the building only add to your doubts about the correctness of the decision to come here. But what if this is the only place where you can be helped...
main::collection_relic_congrats: Congratulations! You have completed the collection!
main::Head: Head
main::intro_cutscene_mayor_1_1: So, here it is, the inside of the Innsmouth Museum. It looks enchanting, Mr. Mayor.
main::intro_cutscene_mayor_1_2: I'm glad you're impressed, Mr. Grum, and that you decided to visit Innsmouth after all. But as you can see, the museum is currently going through challenging times. Due to a rotting roof in most of the halls, there's a lot of moisture. We had to remove the most valuable exhibits to protect them from mold.
main::intro_cutscene_mayor_1_3: It's a bit chilly in here, but don't worry, my foundation has restored buildings in worse condition. Dr. Kraus, how do you find the local exhibits? Aren't they treasures?
main::intro_cutscene_mayor_2_1: Oh? Are you asking me? I'm THRILLED! I can't wait to start my work here. I'll instruct my team to...
main::intro_cutscene_mayor_2_3: Mr. Grum, the museum is on the brink of closure. I would even dare to compare this place to an old and ailing horse that's better off being put down to end its suffering. Most private collectors have far superior collections than what you can find here. Are you sure you're ready to take on its restoration and upkeep? It's madness.
main::intro_cutscene_mayor_3_1: I know what I'm doing. They've called me worse names, my friend. Give me some time, and your town will be known for more than just the cheapest brothels. Ha-ha-ha.
main::intro_cutscene_mayor_3_2: Thank you for your time, Mr. Mayor. I won't keep you any longer. Dr. Kruger and I will explore the museum, and if his assessment is positive, my lawyer will deliver all the necessary documents to you tomorrow.
main::intro_cutscene_mayor_3_3: Thank you, Mr. Grum. You're the only patron who has shown interest in Innsmouth's cultural heritage. I'm just not sure what you're expecting to find in this place. But that's your business now.
main::intro_cutscene_earthquake_1_2: I'm sorry, Head, I thought we agreed that my nickname is Krueger, not Kraus.
main::intro_cutscene_earthquake_1_3: Don't worry. I believe this museum building will be perfect for the Moths.
main::intro_cutscene_earthquake_2_2: This building is about to collapse! Gosh!
main::intro_cutscene_earthquake_2_3: Keep your composure! It's just a minor earthquake. These things happen in Innsmouth. I visited this town with my father when I was young.
main::intro_cutscene_earthquake_3_1: As you say, Head... but what was that noise?
main::intro_cutscene_earthquake_3_2: You need to get out of the laboratory more often, Brain. I'm sure it's just a ship's siren leaving Innsmouth's harbor.
main::intro_cutscene_earthquake_3_3: Gather your people and see what interesting exhibits The Moths can find here.
main::intro_cutscene_hand_story_1_1: Greetings, Head... um... there's something we need to discuss...
main::intro_cutscene_hand_story_1_2: What happened? You look all dirty.
main::intro_cutscene_hand_story_1_3: It's better to discuss matters in private. Brain, aren't you supposed to be dealing with test tubes in the basement or something?
main::intro_cutscene_hand_story_1_4: Cease your bickering, gentlemen. We don't keep secrets from one another. Remember what the code states?
main::intro_cutscene_hand_story_1_5: Knowledge is light. Moths fly toward the light.
main::intro_cutscene_hand_story_1_6: Alright. Lotus and I visited the village as per your orders. Many of the residents there are not in their right minds. They refused to talk to me. As night fell, we followed a group of fishermen dressed in rags who were heading towards the shore. They were mumbling something about the 'Heart of the Sea' and carrying sacks.
main::intro_cutscene_hand_story_1_7: Approaching the ocean, they boarded a boat and dropped those sacks near some sort of huge rock sticking out from the surface of the ocean. A thunderstorm began, and the fishermen returned to the village.
main::intro_cutscene_hand_story_1_8: Lotus and I decided to use the same boat to get a closer look at the rock. Suddenly, as we approached the boat, attackers emerged from nowhere. There were many more of them, and I can swear they were inhuman.
main::intro_cutscene_hand_story_1_9: We had to flee,  and something very strange happened there. I'm certain it was some kind of cult ritual. But I have no idea who those attackers were.
main::intro_cutscene_hand_story_2_1: Calm down, Hand. You did everything right. Don't jump to conclusions.
main::intro_cutscene_hand_story_2_2: Gosh! Hand, why couldn't you have observed them more closely? What if they remember you?
main::intro_cutscene_hand_story_2_3: He did everything right. Go get some rest. You've done a great job. Tomorrow morning, assemble a larger group from our society members and head back to the protrusion. Brain will also provide some of his people. For now, let's disperse and rest.
main::intro_cutscene_bloody_moon_1: And now, this is truly strange! The moon didn't disappear by morning! Why has it turned red-pink? I don't like this, Head.
main::intro_cutscene_bloody_moon_2: I agree. My wise father has records of this in his journals. Let's wait for Hand and his team.
main::intro_cutscene_bloody_monolith_1_1: Here's the rock the fishermen were talking about.
main::intro_cutscene_bloody_monolith_1_2: My people are already examining it. And it's not just a rock, it's a monolith! I can't yet say what the monolith was doing at the bottom of the ocean, but it's definitely not a simple rock. It's covered in symbols of an ancient language. But that's not the strangest part. Inside the monolith, we detected signs of a very powerful force, the nature of which I'll need to investigate further!
main::intro_cutscene_bloody_monolith_1_3: And I can't wait to begin my research. Head, if this monolith holds any secrets, I will uncover them. I never tire of repeating: 'There are no unknowable things if you're willing to accept that the answer may be far more insane.' I'm so excited, it's as if Christmas came early this year.
main::intro_cutscene_bloody_monolith_2_1: Go ahead, Brain. Do your magic.
main::intro_cutscene_bloody_monolith_3_1: Now, regarding the moon. According to my father's journals, the 'Bloody Moon' is a sign of a new era approaching. An era of a new order and the coming of the Elder Gods. My father didn't speak idly. So, I'm beginning to lean towards the idea that our arrival in Innsmouth is not in vain.
main::intro_cutscene_bloody_monolith_3_2: I've already heard this from a local. Though they may not be talkative, I can get anyone talking. The local claims to have seen amphibious people with bulging eyes and scales all over their bodies. He says they are messengers of the gods.
main::intro_cutscene_bloody_monolith_3_3: Please try to avoid using force whenever possible... You know I don't encourage it.
main::intro_cutscene_bloody_monolith_3_4: So, this is no longer mere gossip or jokes. We stand on the brink of something great and incomprehensible. What's happening now, what everything is leading to, was one of my father's greatest fears. He hoped this day would never come. But I'm ready to face this battle. I won't shame the memory of my grandfather and father.
main::intro_cutscene_bloody_monolith_3_5: We need to establish ourselves in Innsmouth and set up our headquarters here. Please go visit the mayor and inform him that the museum is already under restoration.
main::intro_cutscene_bloody_monolith_3_6: But before that, we need new people. Volunteers who are eager to uncover the truth. New Moths members!
main::monolith_cutscene_start_1: Head, it's good that you're here. We haven't been able to decipher the inscriptions yet. The ancient language specialist insists that these symbols don't resemble any known language, and most likely, it's a dead language older than Sumerian.
main::monolith_cutscene_start_2: That's not all. We were fortunate to discover something very peculiar. Something inside the monolith emits low-frequency pulses in a binary rhythm. The nature of these pulses is completely incomprehensible to us.
main::monolith_cutscene_body_1: Bad news, sir. The new recruit didn't return on time. I sent a search party to assist him. Unfortunately, when the search party found him, it was too late. He was already lifeless. We brought his body here to honor his contribution to our cause and bid him farewell with honors.
main::monolith_cutscene_body_2: Poor soul. After death, his face froze in an inhuman grimace from the horrors he witnessed on his journey. Good folks, please prepare a pyre for the cremation of the unfortunate.
main::monolith_cutscene_over_1: Is it over? I think we have nothing more to fear. I've never seen anything like this before!
main::monolith_cutscene_over_2: Look at the face of the deceased. It's no longer distorted by an inhuman grimace. Perhaps, crazy as it may sound, the monolith absorbed all the horror he experienced.
main::monolith_cutscene_over_3: Brain, your hypothesis is not crazy at all. My father wrote about it in his journals. This artifact consumes the fear and dreadful pain experienced by humans. In return, it provides immense power that can be harnessed. Didn't you just witness that?
main::monolith_cutscene_over_4: Head, we need to study it in more detail before using it. We don't fully understand what we're dealing with.
main::monolith_cutscene_over_5: Brain, look outside at the blood moon. Isn't that a sign? What if the monolith is our ONLY SALVATION from the cosmic threat looming over this doomed town? What if we have to fight the Elders? Fight the very gods? Gods whose mere thought drives the most experienced members of our secret society insane?
main::monolith_cutscene_over_6: You can count on me! I'm with you to the end.
main::allies_cutscene_yard_1_1: Innsmouth is silent... This town hides its secrets as if nothing is happening here.
main::allies_cutscene_yard_1_2: This town feels like an abandoned grave. My people are disappearing and dying one by one, often at the hands of the locals.
main::allies_cutscene_yard_2_1: As painful as it is to admit, some people are hardly distinguishable from all those strange creatures that Innsmouth and its surroundings are teeming with. This monolith... It gives us power. But it may not be enough.
main::allies_cutscene_yard_2_2: Head, the monolith indeed holds great power, we've all seen it, but could it not eventually cause more harm than good?
main::allies_cutscene_yard_2_3: The choice is not always ours, Hand. But the consequences of the choice belong to us.
main::allies_cutscene_yard_2_4: We need to secure the support of someone local. Without our people among them, we'll keep falling into their traps. Innsmouth reeks of human indifference and treachery. The moment you turn your back on someone, you'll get a knife in it.
main::allies_cutscene_yard_2_5: My informants keep mentioning "The Weirdo". They claim his influence and connections would be invaluable for the Moths. But my people don't know exactly who he is or what he looks like.
main::allies_cutscene_yard_2_7: Come with me! If you want something done right, do it yourself. Let's take a walk.
main::allies_cutscene_bar_1_1: What are we doing in this filthy bar?
main::allies_cutscene_bar_1_2: Freeing oneself from darkness can be just as important as finding answers. You said your people don't know what the Weirdo looks like. If we can't reach him, maybe he'll want to introduce himself to us.
main::allies_cutscene_bar_2_1: Bartender, we're not here for a drink. I'm sure your bar is a den for people of questionable reputation. You know who I'm talking about.
main::allies_cutscene_bar_2_2: You're not looking for trouble, are you? Neither are we. So maybe you know where we can find the person we're looking for?
main::allies_cutscene_bar_3_1: Gentlemen, I'd gladly watch the locals teach two wealthy rats some manners. But I don't think you'd enjoy it. Hold your horses. Life here is chaotic enough as it is.
main::allies_cutscene_bar_3_2: He's right, Hand. True battles often take place not on the streets but within ourselves. Please, don't threaten anyone.
main::allies_cutscene_bar_4_2: Without your interference, everything here is already spiraling into the abyss. Better save your strength and accept the harsh reality. Why do you think anyone cares about your problems?
main::allies_cutscene_bar_5_2: Who? If anything is weird here, it's two obviously non-local gentlemen asking dangerous questions. I'm a policeman. If you don't want to see the morning, go ahead and do whatever you came to do. But I won't lift a finger to stop the fight. You'll just give me more paperwork to fill out...
main::allies_cutscene_bar_5_3: Thank you, officer. And forgive my partner, he had a bit too much to drink. We won't cause any more trouble. My name is Mr. Grum. Feel free to drop by the Innsmouth Museum sometime. We'll always be happy to see you there. Bartender, treat the officer to the best whiskey on my tab.
main::allies_cutscene_bar_6_1: Head? What was that? Are you going to let him talk to us like that?
main::allies_cutscene_bar_6_2: We've done what we came for. The Weirdo will know where to find us.
main::allies_cutscene_museum_1_1: What are you snooping around for in my laboratory, girl? Haven't you been taught that all reckless actions have consequences?
main::allies_cutscene_museum_1_2: What are you up to here? What is this strange monolith? And who are you people anyway? Some dirty cult?
main::allies_cutscene_museum_1_3: The discovery of truths better left unknown often makes people regret it. And when you're not ready to accept the truth, it can drive you mad!
main::allies_cutscene_museum_2_2: Some snoop broke into my lab, Head. Consider her gone, I'll take care of her...
main::allies_cutscene_museum_2_3: No, Brain. This miss hasn't done anything wrong... Her thirst for answers led her to us... Like a moth to the light. To those like her.
main::allies_cutscene_museum_3_2: All in due time, miss. I will explain everything to you later. If you are ready to walk through the darkness, you can join us. But be careful... sometimes it's best not to search for the shadows.
main::tension_cutscene_museum_1_1: Miss Julia, it's a pity you don't want to join our society. Your determination and skills would be useful to us.
main::tension_cutscene_museum_1_2: Our views may be similar, but each of us has our own goal. You're a wise person, surely you realize that I wasn't snooping around the museum on the day of our meeting without reason.
main::tension_cutscene_museum_1_3: I know. I know much more than you think. Including the disappearance of your sister.
main::tension_cutscene_museum_1_4: What? How do you know about that? Do you know something about her fate?
main::tension_cutscene_museum_1_5: I know a lot, though not everything, of course. In a world ruled by shadows, the path to the light is very thorny. Maybe your path will become easier in the company of like-minded people.
main::tension_cutscene_museum_2_1: But do you really want to know YOUR answer?
main::tension_cutscene_yard_1_1: The monolith is becoming a source of power that we desperately need. By absorbing the horrors and torments of the dead, it makes us more prepared to face truly serious dangers.
main::tension_cutscene_yard_1_2: The secrets of the monolith elude both me and my scientists. Head, you somehow knew that there would be work for us in Innsmouth.
main::tension_cutscene_yard_1_3: But these fears... This is more than just energy. It could be something far greater than we can imagine. My team is still racking their brains, hoping to grasp the essence of this strange artifact. We are playing with forces that could consume us.
main::tension_cutscene_yard_1_4: The Head knows what he's doing. This is our chance. Every sacrifice is a step toward hope. We can't stop halfway.
main::tension_cutscene_yard_2_1: Well, well, what a pleasant company we've gathered here. Quite the show you put on at the bar. So, anyone in need of special services? I can get you anything you need, and more...
main::tension_cutscene_yard_2_2: But every service has its fair price.
main::tension_cutscene_yard_2_3: Greetings, Weirdo. We are The Moths. I knew you'd be interested in our offer. And I'm sure we will be useful to each other. But we're less interested in services, and more in information.
main::tension_cutscene_yard_3_1: Head... he's a monster. Do you really trust him?
main::tension_cutscene_yard_4_1: Don't trust that creature! His services might turn out to be more dangerous than useful. The world will end anyway. Your attempts to change anything are so pathetic that they make me sick.
main::tension_cutscene_yard_5_1: Everyone has their price and place in this world. You only see part of the picture. And our goal requires actions that others might consider cruel.
main::tension_cutscene_yard_5_2: First the monolith, now working with a monster. We must be careful, Head. We shouldn't blindly follow something that could lead to our demise.
main::tension_cutscene_yard_6_1: Well, my brainy friend, it seems your concerns aren't entirely unfounded. But YOU sought me out. Here I am. Mr. Grum. I'm a businessman, just like you. My word is as good as gold.
main::tension_cutscene_yard_6_2: Fellowship, we are playing a game with the highest stakes. Sometimes, to win, you have to take risks and rely on forces that may seem too dark for your understanding.
main::betrayal_cutscene_museum_1_1: The Monolith... it can't take it! This is more than it can absorb.
main::betrayal_cutscene_museum_1_2: Now's not the time for doubts, idiot. We're approaching the peak, when everything will fall into place.
main::betrayal_cutscene_museum_1_3: But what place is that, Head? The artifact is very unstable! We need to stop the experiment! Now!
main::betrayal_cutscene_museum_1_4: You all only see fragments... But I see the entire canvas. Soon he will awaken...
main::betrayal_cutscene_museum_1_5: Who will awaken? Head... you're dragging us into the abyss!
main::betrayal_cutscene_museum_1_6: All that was hidden will now be revealed! Zal'gat mor'it val'nar!
main::betrayal_cutscene_museum_2_1: What have we done... My faith... was just a mirage. Head! You've betrayed us! And you've betrayed me!
main::betrayal_cutscene_museum_2_2: I was sure I was getting close to understanding its structure... But in reality, I was only playing a game I didn’t understand. I have no idea how these forces work. What do I even know?
main::betrayal_cutscene_museum_scene_b_1: The Head pretended to be light, and it blinded us. But now that light is out. Now we are left in darkness.

It was all for nothing... We are destroying the world ourselves... For what? And now the portal is open. What will come through? More creatures? What will be left for us? Only the shattered remains of what we once were.

I always sought to know more. Maybe only through darkness can we truly understand the world. Give me his journals. I want to dive into the dark knowledge that clouded the Head's mind. Maybe they hold some clues.
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_1_1: The Head wanted to awaken someone. Who? What if these forces are so powerful that the world was ALREADY doomed?
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_1_2: I can explain anything, but what I see... it doesn’t fit any framework. It’s something beyond reason. We've awakened forces we were never meant to touch.
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_1_3: I’ve been studying the Head's father's journals for weeks. There's a similar situation described. There's some small hope... a chance to close the portal for good. But it requires a great sacrifice.
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_1_4: What kind of sacrifice? Killing a dozen innocent people? No! There must be a solution that doesn't involve spilling innocent blood. But I no longer believe in our success. Maybe Grim was right all along. The world is spiraling into the abyss.
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_2_1: You want to give up? Is that all you're capable of? You all came here seeking change. And now, on the brink of something incredible, you tuck your tails?
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_2_2: Didn't you feel how this has changed you? Open wounds have turned to scars, but scars don't just represent pain. They show that you survived. So, do you really want to give up now, when these scars are already a part of you?
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_3_1: What are you so afraid of? The monsters at the gate? We all have our monsters INSIDE... Each of us has been changed by what we've endured. And some of us have changed for the worse. But isn't giving up now just another form of betrayal to ourselves?
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_4_1: What do you understand? You all... are involved in horrors I couldn't even imagine. You're murderers. You took my sister. And you expect me to be on your side?
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_4_2: I'm leaving. You've brought this on yourselves. Burn in hell! You and your pathetic society of losers!
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_5_1: Hand, the ritual I speak of. It requires more than just any sacrifice. And I'm afraid it cannot be made. The sacrifice must be something or someone truly cherished by the person who opened the portal. I can't even imagine what that could be.
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_5_2: The Head lied to us this whole time, he turned out to be worse than any of the monsters that now roam around. I doubt there’s anything in this world that he cared about besides his obsession with revenge.
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_5_3: He betrayed us. The Head had his demons, and now we've seen where that led. I never felt this helpless, not even during the war...
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_6_1: This is our trial... Reality is crumbling around us. But if the Head taught me anything, it's to see it through to the end, even if all the odds are against us. Even if it's TOTAL madness.
main::last_hope_cutscene_museum_6_2: You humans... you always ruin everything. Can't you see? You ARE the cause of this catastrophe. You've destroyed everything around and are dragging me into your grave.
main::final_cutscene_museum_1_1: Never in my life have I been as wrong as when I underestimated the power of occult sciences. Kar'vot ul'nar. I'm as excited as I was in my freshman year at Harvard. I can master so much new knowledge.
main::final_cutscene_museum_1_2: I and my scientists... err... I mean my ACOLYTES... still can't get used to this... are ready to perform the ancient ritual. A ritual that is supposed to close the cursed portal. The creatures will no longer be able to enter Earth. But without a sacrifice, the ritual may fail.
main::final_cutscene_museum_2_1: My recruits, like hundreds of bees, are striking at Cthulhu. He no longer seems so invulnerable. It's costing us dearly, but I believe this might work...
main::final_cutscene_museum_2_2: After analyzing the Head's entire life, Lotus and I came to the conclusion that if the Head cherished anything, it was the memories of his father. His journals must be the key to this. He drew information from them, he always kept them with him. These journals must be sacrificed. And bury this dark knowledge forever.
main::final_cutscene_museum_3_2: I won't let you ruin everything! You were all just my pawns. My plan is nearly complete, and it’s not for you, miserable fools, to decide what must happen... The fate of the world is already sealed. And now... everything will end as it was meant to.
main::final_cutscene_museum_4_1: Hand, it was foolish of you not to flee. Unfortunately, now I’ll have to deal with you as well.
main::final_cutscene_museum_5_1: I couldn't save my soldiers... but now... I will save you.
main::final_cutscene_museum_8_1: This is for my sister, you bastard!
main::final_cutscene_museum_scene_b_1_1: This is what I was always seeking... Mysteries that never succumbed to logic. Now I can control them.
main::final_cutscene_museum_scene_b_3_1: Congratulations, my friend. We tend to think our actions fade into the whirl of time, leaving no trace. But there are moments when a single act can change the course of the world, as if the entire universe had been waiting for it.
main::act1_description: The Terrifying Suburbs of Innsmouth. Populated by hostile locals and a small number of extraterrestrial beings.
main::act2_description: Beyond the Innsmouth conglomeration lies the wild lands, teeming with a large number of extraterrestrial creatures.
main::act3_description: A region already permeated with entities from other worlds. It's difficult to say to which world this horrifying realm belongs.
main::travel_card_fight_description: A brawl with hostile local residents
main::travel_card_monster_fight_descript: A skirmish with beings from other worlds
main::travel_card_gas_station_descriptio: Visit to replenish your supply of fuel <sprite name="fuel"> for the journey
main::travel_card_motel_description: Stop by the motel to restore HP <sprite name="damage">, upgrade <sprite name="card"> or get a consumable.
main::travel_card_treasure_description: Obtain a relic or a powerful <sprite name="card">
main::travel_card_warehouse_description: Visit the storehouse to steal/buy <sprite name="wood"> <sprite name="bricks"> <sprite name="steel"> for upgrading the Secret Society
main::travel_card_asylum_description: Visit the asylum to restore sanity <sprite name="sanity"> or insight <sprite name="insight">
main::cards_till_boss_fight: cards to play till the Boss Fight
main::travel_demo_end_title: This character progress ends here in the demo version
main::travel_demo_end_text: Don't worry. You've done everything as best as you could. It's just the end of the character's journey, not the end of the demo.
main::travel_demo_end_merchant: Sorry, friend, but for now, it's a no-go. The time has not come yet... Hahaha.
main::encounter_lighthouse_description:
While passing by an old lighthouse through thick fog, its keeper rushes towards you. 

 It's a weathered old man, claiming that the lighthouse has gone dark because the lantern oil has run out. 

 If you don't take immediate action, ships might run aground. He asks you to go inside and maintain the fire with wood until he returns with fuel.
main::encounter_lighthouse_option_accept:
You agree to help and enter the lighthouse. After a short while, the keeper indeed returns, but not by himself, accompanied by a group of stern locals. 

 Prepare for a battle.
main::encounter_lighthouse_option_ignore: You walk past the imploring keeper. He shouts curses at you in tears.
main::encounter_lighthouse_option_run_re: You agree to help and run to a nearby village for oil. Upon returning to the lighthouse, you don't see any sign of the keeper. Upon inspecting the lighthouse from the inside, you notice that it has been abandoned and unused for a long time.
main::encounter_lighthouse_option_choke_:
You pretend to offer help to the poor old man. As you approach, you grab him in a bear hug and start choking him. Although the old man is quite elderly, he still delivers powerful blows to your torso.

But eventually, he loses consciousness. You check to see if there’s anything valuable in his pockets...
main::encounter_ferryman_description:
The river obstructs your path. Grey clouds have veiled the sky, and the wind has picked up. The air is heavy with the scent of marshes. 

 Occasionally, thunder rumbles in the silence. On the riverbank, near a crackling fire, you see a man mending a wooden raft.
main::encounter_ferryman_option_favor_te: Ask for a ride across the river
main::encounter_ferryman_option_favor_re:
You ask the man if he can ferry you across the river. 

 Reluctantly, he agrees but asks for 25 coins for his help. He beckons you to a boat nearby and takes you across the dark river, with waves growing higher as a storm approaches.
main::encounter_ferryman_option_bridge_r:
You inquire about the closest crossing from the man. 

 Unfortunately, according to him, the bridge is an hour away from this place. The man suggests waiting out the possible rain by his fire and offers you tea.
main::encounter_ferryman_option_steal_ra:
ou set up an ambush on the riverbank, hiding in the tall reeds, waiting for the moment when the man leaves the raft unattended. 

 After stealing the raft, you hastily row across the ice-cold water, nervously gripping your hands in the frigid depths.  

 The raft, which the man didn't have time to fix because of you, begins to fall apart in the middle of the river. In the end, you must jump into the water and swim to shore unassisted.
main::encounter_ferryman_option_ask_rod_:
You approach the man, who is a bit startled by your sudden appearance, and offer to share a drink of moonshine with him. The man eagerly agrees.

Afterward, you ask for permission to fish. After a while, you catch a very modest haul, which catches the stranger's attention. Grateful for the drink and the company, he puts the catch he had recently caught on the fire.

The warm dinner makes you feel much better!
main::encounter_traffic_description:
It's an exceptionally dark and cold night as you walk down the road, with only dense woods on either side. 

 A few hundred meters ahead, you spot a beam of light on the road. Suddenly, a horrific and putrid odor fills your nose. In the distance, from the direction of the ocean, a very low and ominous rumble, reminiscent of a ship's siren, can be heard. 

 Carefully advancing, you see that the beam of light is coming from the headlight of a car with open windows. The other headlight is shattered. In the light of the headlights, you see two bodies lying on the ground.
main::encounter_traffic_option_ask_help_:
Upon seeing a house with visible lights on the horizon, you rush toward it. 

 In haste, you explain to the homeowner what happened, and together, you both return to the scene as quickly as possible. 

 However, upon your return, you find that there is no one left to help. The bodies of the three unknown individuals have been stripped down to the bone.
main::encounter_traffic_option_look_for_: Check if they have anything valuable

You begin to search the bodies for anything valuable. Out of the corner of your eye, you notice slow movement. One of the victims reaches out to you, faintly whispering, 'please...' 

 On his finger, you notice an unusually shaped ring. Trying not to look into the man's eyes, you take the ring and run away as fast as you can.
main::encounter_traffic_option_start_eng:
Peering into the car, you see that it's covered in blood and sticky, foamy slime inside. 

 Unfortunately, the car doesn't start. However, in the glove compartment, you find something wrapped in a cloth. The horrifying, low growl that you previously thought was a distant siren is now much closer. You grab the item wrapped in the cloth and flee with all your might.
main::encounter_traffic_option_investiga:
You decide to examine the site of the accident. As you inspect the wounds on the bodies of the deceased, you realize that the injuries resemble claw or bite marks more than anything else. Strange...

Suddenly, something slimy starts dripping on you from above, from the treetops. Your heart begins to race wildly. Slowly, you look up and see dozens of eyes in the treetops. You dash back to your car...

What were those creatures?
main::encounter_cultists_description: Passing by the farm at sunset, you see people dressed in animal skins. They are loudly laughing and shouting strange words as they light a large bonfire in the middle of the field and begin to worship the fire.
main::encounter_cultists_option_lay_low_: Lay low and see what happens next

Unable to contain your curiosity, you hide behind large stones. After more strange chants, the villagers take out knives.  

 Suddenly, each one slits their own throat. Without ceasing their laughter, they fall to the ground. 

 Complete silence follows. The bonfire flares up twice as bright, and a horrifying monstrosity emerges from it.
main::encounter_cultists_option_stop_rit: Suspecting that the cultists are planning something terrible, you shout at them to stop. A chase begins, but you won't get far. Prepare for a fight!
main::encounter_cultists_option_turn_aro: Turn around and cautiously go back

Not taking your eyes off the bizarre ritual, you stealthily step backward, unaware that a wolf trap lies beneath your feet. 

 The trap snaps shut, and you, suppressing a scream of pain, carefully extract your leg from it.
main::encounter_cultists_option_open_cag:
You notice something shiny lying on the ground near the cultists. You decide to open your bird cage and release the crow, hoping it will bring back the mysterious, shiny object. The crow takes flight...

After some time, it returns. It hasn’t brought anything shiny, but it didn't come back alone. Another crow has arrived with it.
main::encounter_monster_child_descriptio:
Passing by the ruins of an abandoned house on the riverbank, you hear quiet bubbling sounds resembling singing, as well as tapping noises. 

 You examine a pile of debris and bricks from which the sounds are coming. Slowly emerging from it is a child dressed in tattered rags who gazes at you. 

 The creature's body and face are partially covered in scales. A pair of large, round, black eyes fixate on you. Without showing fear towards you, the creature approaches and extends its hands.
main::encounter_monster_child_option_att: You pretend not to pose a threat to the strange creature. As you take another step toward it, you suddenly strike the creature in the head with a brick, using all your strength.
main::encounter_monster_child_option_ext: Extend your hand to the creature

Nervously, you reach your hand out towards the creature while preparing to pull it back quickly in case of danger. 

 The child places its hand on top of yours and gives your hand a gentle squeeze. Then, it turns around and continues crawling away, singing something in an incomprehensible language.
main::encounter_monster_child_option_exa: Examine what the monster was interested in

Scaring the creature away with your cries, you go to see what had piqued its interest so much. 

 Prepared for anything, you peer into the pile and find materials in good condition.
main::encounter_monster_child_option_giv: Throw some money to the Creature

You interpret the creature's gesture as a request for something. You dig through your pockets and find a few coins, which you drop into the hand of the strange child. The creature hisses and slinks toward the water. It points with its large eyes at a rock and then disappears beneath the surface.

After looking around, you muster the courage to enter the water and investigate where the creature pointed.

There’s indeed something lying in the water...
main::encounter_looting_bodies_descripti:
At dawn, you continue your journey. 

 The night was restless. In this perilous and malevolent place, every dawn is a rebirth. You never know if you'll wake up tomorrow. 

 Walking along the railroad tracks, you spot a figure crawling among the lifeless bodies. They've had less luck than you. You crouch slightly to avoid being noticed and try to understand what's happening. Suddenly, the figure turns in your direction. It's a merchant! 

 What?
main::encounter_looting_bodies_option_co: Condemn the merchant for scavenging among the dead.

You realize the person before you is a merchant, and you're disgusted by the thought that he's looting the dead. 

 That's where he gets his goods. Engaging in a verbal altercation, the merchant just smiles. 

 'Since when did the Moths become such moralists? There's enough for everyone here. Hahaha!' the merchant responds and tosses a bag at your feet.
main::encounter_looting_bodies_option_ex: Try to examine how the people died.

Silently passing by the scene of a terrible atrocity, you attempt to see what happened here. The faces of the deceased reflect a cosmic horror experienced in their last moments of life. Even the appearance of these faces sends shivers down your spine. 

  The merchant leaves without uttering a word. One of the dead is clutching a handful of coins. 

 Why didn't the merchant take them?
main::encounter_looting_bodies_option_li: Lie flat on the ground to avoid being noticed

You pressed yourself to the ground so that traces of dirt appeared on your face. The merchant returned to his business. You noticed that he was placing something in the hands of the deceased. 

 Lowering your gaze, you saw a well-dressed man in black right in front of you. Next to him lay a book. When you grabbed it, you backed away and cast one last glance at the merchant. 

 There was something stirring in his sack...
main::encounter_looting_bodies_option_bl:
Recognizing the merchant, you shout:

- So that's where you get your goods...

The merchant suddenly spins around with an inhuman hiss, towering over you at one and a half times your height. Something beastly in his eyes fills you with wild, primal fear. Realizing you are a member of the secret society, the merchant relaxes, rummages through his cloak, and tosses a piece of paper your way, saying:

- Come again! Hahaha. You'll get a discount...
main::encounter_gentelemen_description:
Among the abandoned houses of the fishing village, you come across two men in dirty and torn clothes. Seeing you, they rise from their creaky chairs, and one of them says: 

- Welcome, Mr.(Miss) Rich Man. Hahaha. You must be very tired from the road. Would you like to offer me and my friend some tobacco? Hahaha. 

They don't look very dangerous, but they surround you from different sides. You smell the stench of their breath.
main::encounter_gentelemen_option_start_: Understanding what's coming, you decide to attack first. A brawl ensues. After knocking both to the ground, you look around and notice a small metal canister. Grabbing the canister, you run to your car and drive away as fast as you can. It's only on the road that you realize you lost something in the heat of the fight.
main::encounter_gentelemen_option_offer_:
You display your hunting knife to the suspicious men, hinting that it's better not to mess with you. The men take a few steps back. You take out a few banknotes and offer them in exchange for a peaceful search of a nearby house. The man who was mocking you earlier says: 

- You're a person I'd like to do business with. Give me that money. Hahaha.
main::encounter_gentelemen_option_push_t:
You push one of the men away. The other tries to grab you. After dealing with the second one, both men run in different directions, laughing loudly. 

But something's wrong. Patting your pockets, you realize you've been robbed.
main::encounter_gentelemen_option_give_t:
Without hesitation and remembering that you have a pouch of tobacco with you, you offer it to the men. They seem a bit surprised and confused but start laughing.

The tension between you eases. The men invite you to join them. One of them pulls out an open bottle of gin and hands it to you.

After spending a couple of hours in amusing conversation, the thought crosses your mind that you shouldn't be a judge. A renewed faith in humanity warms your soul.
main::encounter_church_description:
Late at night, you pass by a small church with lights on inside. Tired from your difficult journey and realizing it's late, you decide to ask for shelter until morning. 

Inside, you are greeted by a holy father who, after a long conversation, offers you confession.
main::encounter_church_option_decline_re:
You decline the confession but decide to leave a donation to the church for the shelter provided. 

In gratitude, the holy father points to a room where you can rest until morning.
main::encounter_church_option_confess_si:
You enter the confession room and begin to repent for all the wrongs you've done over the past years. For all the pain you've caused to strangers. 

At the end of the confession, you feel mentally exhausted, but this event has opened your eyes to reality.
main::encounter_church_option_imaginary_: You enter the confession room and begin to repent for imaginary sins, adding disgusting details. Playing a role that's not yours, you only later realize how oppressed the holy father became from your lies. At some point, he screams and kicks you out of the church onto the street. But you manage to grab one "trifle" with you.
main::encounter_church_option_change_top:
Feeling uncomfortable refusing the priest, you ask him for directions to the nearest gas station instead. The priest, visibly disappointed, points you to the door after a long pause. As you leave, he gives you a firm hug and says he will pray for your sinful soul.

Already on the road, you realize you can’t find your wallet... Could that hug have been just a cover for theft?
main::encounter_antique_shop_description:
The day was cloudy and cold. Walking down a long deserted street of a town, you come across an antique shop with its door open.

Inside, you are greeted by an old woman who offers to show you her strange goods from around the world. One particular item catches your attention...
main::encounter_antique_shop_option_purp:
You are intrigued by the flask with a strangely looking liquid.

The woman eagerly brings it to you, uncorks it, and a purple gas fills the air, smelling like perfume.

You lose consciousness and wake up only after a few hours. The shop is empty.
main::encounter_antique_shop_option_box_: Box in the dark corner of the room

You are interested in the box in the dark corner of the room. You ask the woman about its contents.

"Dip your hand into the box and whatever you find, you may take." she replies mysteriously, with a barely noticeable smile.

You plunge your hand into the box and grab an unknown object. But suddenly, you feel unbearable pain, as if thousands of needles have pricked you.
main::encounter_antique_shop_option_toba:
You are interested in the exotic tobacco. Smiling, the woman gives it to you for free. You insist on paying, but in response, she says, "One day I will receive payment from you...".

You step outside and enjoy the aromatic tobacco.
main::encounter_antique_shop_option_card:
You're intrigued by the fortune-telling card deck. The woman excitedly fans out the deck and offers you to pick any card in exchange for one randomly selected from your own deck.

Just before you can flip the chosen card, she adds in a mysterious voice, 'Whatever is depicted on this card—it will be the cause of your demise...'
main::encounter_collector_description:
As you drive past farmlands, you notice an old man standing by the roadside. He is dressed in expensive clothes and has a very large suitcase with him. He gestures for you to give him a ride. Seeing nothing threatening, you stop your car and help load his luggage onto the roof.

During the conversation on the road, you learn that the old man collects various antiques. The man asks if you have anything interesting in terms of antiquities.
main::encounter_collector_option_lion_fi:
You take out the lion figurine. The old man excitedly takes it in his hands and quickly puts on his glasses to examine it more closely.

For the next hour of the journey, the man cheerfully talks about how he used to collect such figurines. At one of the crossroads, he asks you to stop as he needs to go in a different direction. As a token of gratitude for the ride, he pulls out another figurine from his suitcase and hands it to you. It's an eagle figurine.
main::encounter_collector_option_cats_mu:
You take out the cat mummy. The old man excitedly takes it in his hands and quickly puts on his glasses to examine it more closely.

For the next hour of the journey, the man cheerfully talks about how he used to collect Egyptian artifacts. At one of the crossroads, he asks you to stop as he needs to go in a different direction. As a token of gratitude for the ride, he pulls out a small golden pyramid from his suitcase and hands it to you.
main::encounter_collector_option_silver_:
You take out the silver coin. The old man excitedly takes it in his hands and quickly puts on his glasses to examine it more closely.

For the next hour of the journey, the man cheerfully talks about how he used to be a numismatist. At one of the crossroads, he asks you to stop as he needs to go in a different direction. As a token of gratitude for the ride, he pulls out a rope from his suitcase and hands it to you, adding: "It's just an ordinary rope… not ancient… but maybe it will come in handy."
main::encounter_collector_option_decline:
You tell the old man that you're not interested in old junk. This makes him a bit disappointed, but he doesn't show any sign of being offended.

You continue to drive in silence for another hour. At one of the crossroads, he asks you to stop as he needs to go in a different direction. He shakes your hand in gratitude for the ride, takes his suitcase, and disappears into the darkness.
main::encounter_mysterious_stranger_desc:
Stopping for the night at yet another motel, you once again notice that an unknown figure is constantly watching you, appearing only in the shadows.

You had noticed this figure before, but reassured yourself that it was just paranoia.

This situation has gone too far; you need to do something right now. It's unknown what threats the mysterious stalker might pose.
main::encounter_mysterious_stranger_opti:
You realize it's now or never and rush towards the stranger.

The stranger suddenly turns around the corner of the building. You chase after him with all your might and barely catch a glimpse of the dark figure entering one of the motel rooms and closing the door behind them.

You burst into the room, but it's empty. The stranger has evaded you.

You decide to ambush the mysterious stalker. Going around the corner of the building and hiding behind a streetlamp, you wait for the right moment to pounce on the mysterious pursuer.

Half a minute later, someone grabs you from behind with a cold, stone-like hand. You're thrown to the ground, your arm twisted behind your back. You signal to the stranger that you surrender. To which you hear the response: "You hunt shadows, forgetting about the light. Your weakest trait is distinguishing allies from enemies."

After this, the stranger disappears into the darkness. You will never see him again.

You jump into your car and drive as fast as you can, in complete darkness, without even turning on the headlights. After driving a dozen miles, you turn onto a dirt road and continue for some time.

Sleeping in the car in the middle of nowhere isn't the best rest, but you feel much calmer now. The mysterious stranger has lost track of you.

You decide that you've read too many detective novels, and the events of the past few days have thrown you off balance. You head to your room and go to sleep, trying to get rid of paranoid thoughts.

In the morning, you unexpectedly find a package at your door. Looking around, you see no one. Taking the package to your room, you find a note inside: "The choice is simple: save us or betray everyone."
main::encounter_circus_description:
A note lies on the windshield of your car, inviting you to a private performance at an old circus. Despite your skepticism, you decide to go.

When you arrive, the circus looks abandoned, but for a moment, lights flicker inside, and you hear music that sounds carnival-like, though some notes are off-key.

The music feels more ominous than entertaining.
main::encounter_circus_option_music_sour: Investigate the source of the music

You decide to find out where the eerie music is coming from. Following the sound, you discover a gramophone. Gathering your courage, you play your own record and dance to the lively swing tempo.

It reminds you of the good old days.
main::encounter_circus_option_invitation:
You decide to look at the invitation you found earlier. But after searching your pockets, you realize it’s missing.

Was the invitation just a figment of your imagination? Trying to recall the moment you found the letter, you only feel a headache.

Coming back to your senses, you see that the music and lights are gone. Maybe you're starting to lose your mind.
main::encounter_circus_option_look_for_p:
You slowly move through the circus tent, trying to find someone. Someone must have turned on the lights and the creepy music.

Calling out, "Is anyone here?" you hear a rustling behind the stands. You freeze, hearing quick but very quiet footsteps. Something small is moving towards you in the dark.

Peering into the darkness, you realize there’s no danger. It’s a friendly stray dog. It runs up to you, wagging its tail cheerfully. The tag on its collar reads "Dick."

In good spirits, you turn and head for the exit. After a loud whistle, the dog happily follows you.
main::encounter_circus_option_fortune_te: You notice an old mechanical fortune-teller machine in a dark corner. Finding a quarter in your pocket, you insert it into the machine. Inside, something starts clinking and moving. A few seconds later, a small envelope drops out at the bottom. Inside is a note that reads, "The spirit that doesn’t waver will move even the unmovable."
main::encounter_phone_call_description: Walking down a deserted street to stretch your legs, you come across an abandoned phone booth, its condition indicating it hasn't been used in a long time. Suddenly, the phone starts ringing. You look around... There’s not a soul in sight. Only ominous thunder rolls across the sky. You slowly open the booth door and hesitantly lift the receiver to your ear. A raspy, monotonous voice comes through the phone: "[Name], is that you? You're in great danger! If it’s really you, say the password!"
main::encounter_phone_call_option_knowle:
You say, "Knowledge is light…" and hear the reply, "... Moths fly to the light." It’s someone from the society.

Without saying a word, you wait for a response. The voice on the other end continues, "You need to leave this place immediately... you're being watched! Check the trash bin behind the bookstore. RUN! Right now!"
main::encounter_phone_call_option_secret:
You respond with the first code phrase that comes to mind. The voice on the phone remains silent. After a few seconds, all you hear is the dial tone. The caller has hung up.

Who was it? How do they know your name?
main::encounter_phone_call_option_thats_:
You respond that it’s indeed you. The voice on the phone remains silent.

After a few seconds, all you hear is the dial tone. The caller has hung up.

Who was it? How do they know your name?
main::encounter_phone_call_option_silenc:
Having great suspicions about the caller's intentions, you stay silent. The voice on the phone does the same.

After a few seconds, all you hear is the dial tone. The caller has hung up.

Who was it? How do they know your name?
main::encounter_woman_in_white_descripti: During a night drive along a mountain road, you notice a woman in white waving her arms. Stopping near her, you realize she is dressed in an old-fashioned manner. She runs up to your car window, claiming that she and her mother were attacked by an unknown assailant. She begs for help, but her mother is nowhere to be seen.
main::encounter_woman_in_white_option_ru: Run together in the direction she indicates

Together with the woman, you run in the direction she points to. Over the hill, in the darkness, you can indeed see two figures.

Shouting, "Leave her alone, scoundrel!" you rush towards them. One of the figures starts running away. Having exhausted all your strength to get here, you are unable to give chase.

You drive both women to the nearest hospital. The doctor, seeing your condition, provides assistance to all three of you.
Run alone in the direction she indicates

Wasting no time, you run in the direction the woman pointed to. Over the hill, you indeed see two figures. One of them is lying on the ground and not moving.

Approaching closer, you see a horrific scene. An elderly woman, covered in cuts, has fallen victim to some perverted cult ritual. There is no saving her. The other figure, holding a knife, confidently moves towards you.

Prepare for a fight!
main::encounter_woman_in_white_option_to: Take the woman to the town

You tell the woman to pull herself together and calm down. You order her to get in the car, then speed off towards the town. The woman continues to beg for help for her mother.

As you drive through the town, you notice a police station and stop there. After listening to all the details, the police send a patrol to the place you came from. The officer thanks you for your concern.
main::encounter_woman_in_white_option_as: Ask about the details of the attack

You want to understand the situation better so you know what to expect. Calming the woman down, you try to gather more information. But the woman is hysterical.

Suddenly, the woman runs off in the same direction she appeared from.

What was that? It looks like some sort of trap...
main::encounter_misunderstanding_at_bar_:
You enter a bar to wet your whistle after a long day on the road. After ordering a drink, you notice that people around you are behaving strangely—every gaze is fixed on you, yet no one approaches.

Finally, the bartender hands you a piece of paper with a note: "Are you one of them?".

Realizing the situation is getting out of control, you stand up and head towards the door without even touching your drink. Halfway to the exit, you see that a burly man is blocking the door, and the patrons start surrounding you.

Prepare for a fight!

You nod to the bartender, who then takes the paper, crumples it up, and throws it in the trash. The bartender leaves the room. A heavy tension fills the air.

A few minutes later, the bartender returns with a newspaper in hand and hands it to you, adding: "Tell HIM it won't happen again...".

Finishing your drink, you leave the bar and notice money tucked inside the newspaper.

You confidently point to the Moths Emblem on your chest and down your drink in one gulp. The bartender places both hands on the bar and leans threateningly towards you, glancing around. He then pulls out a hip flask from under the counter and hands it to you, looking you straight in the eye. Seeing confusion in your gaze, he adds, "On the house...".

You make the Hastur Cult Gesture that you saw in one of the Moths Library books. The bartender furrows his brows, exhales loudly, and nods his head as if to signal "Take him!" to the bar patrons.

You quickly jump up, spilling your drink, but a couple of burly men grab you by the arms from behind. After beating you nearly unconscious, they throw you out onto the street.
main::encounter_pairs_description:
You encounter the merchant who shuffles a deck of cards and gives a sly smile. Noticing you, he offers to play a game with him. 

What a strange place and time for a card game. Is he up to something sinister?
main::encounter_pairs_option_confirm_res:
The merchant eagerly invites you over. He starts laying out the cards on the ground... 

- Do you think luck is on your side? Ha-ha-ha. If you win, the cards are yours...
main::@encounter_pairs_reward: A chance to win up to 3 cards
main::encounter_pairs_option_decline_res: You turn away and go about your business. The the merchant watches you leave with a broad smile.
main::encounter_mini_game_pairs_rules: You have 3 attempts to find pairs of cards. In one attempt, you can flip 2 any cards. If these cards match, you will receive this card into your deck at the end of the mini-game.
main::encounter_parcels_description:
You encounter a mailman standing next to a pile of various parcels. Noticing you, he offers to sell you one of the parcels for a symbolic fee. 

What a strange offer. The mailman sees the confusion in your eyes and adds:

- Don't worry, these are "lost" parcels. No one will be looking for them. Hahaha.
main::encounter_parcels_option_confirm_r:
The mailman beckons you closer, selects a few boxes from the pile, and sets them before you... 

- Do you think luck is on your side?
main::@encounter_parcels_reward: A chance to win a relic
main::encounter_parcels_option_decline_r:
You turn around and go about your business. The mailman watches you leave and shouts after you:

- You didn't see me here! Got it?
main::encounter_mini_game_parcels_rules: Choose one of the relics and remember the box it's in. When you're ready, press [Ready]. Be careful! The boxes will start shuffling. Once they have shuffled, select one of them.
main::encounter_smuggling_description:
You come across two suspicious men whose truck broke down in the middle of the road, packed to the brim with suitcases of various sizes and colors.

Noticing you, they become slightly nervous. Given the circumstances, they appear to be smugglers. Stopping beside them, you start a conversation.

- Relax, guys, I'm not with the police. What interesting things do you have here?

The men exchange glances. One of them approaches your car and leans into the window.

- If you've got money, you can take a few.
main::encounter_smuggling_option_confirm: After taking your money, the men toss a few suitcases onto the ground in front of you. You step closer and peek inside each one.
main::encounter_smuggling_option_decline: You start your car and drive off. In the rearview mirror, you see the smugglers making rude gestures in your direction.
main::encounter_mini_game_smuggling_rule: The smugglers have agreed to sell you any number of suitcases for an amount not exceeding [Summ], which you have ALREADY paid. So, choose as many suitcases as your money allows, and when you're ready, press [Confirm]. If a suitcase is selected by mistake, click on it again to deselect it.
main::tutorial_page1_note2_text: Cards require Energy () to play (can be found at top left corner of the card). Once the energy is depleted, end your turn.
main::tutorial_page1_note3_text: Consumables do not require energy to play and can be played at any time during your turn.
main::tutorial_page1_note4_text: You can play an ability as many times per turn as your hero has Insight() points .
main::tutorial_page1_note5_text: You control only your hero character. Allies and enemies act independently. The turn starts with the hero's turn, followed by allies, and then enemies.
main::tutorial_page2_note2_text: The Attack () adds to the Damage () of your next attacking card. At the end of the turn, the Attack disappears completely.
main::tutorial_page2_note3_text: The more attacking cards you play per turn, the larger the Attack Combo Bonus added to the damage of the attacking card. At the beginning of the next turn, all Combo Bonuses are reset.
main::tutorial_page3_note2_text: Block absorbs the damage the enemy intends to inflict on you. At the beginning of the next turn, block residues will be halved.
main::tutorial_page3_note3_text: The more block cards you play per turn, the larger the Block Combo Bonus from applying the block.
main::tutorial_page3_note4_text: In a fight with truly dangerous creatures, the hero may lose Sanity points (). Complete loss of sanity leads to defeat.
main::tutorial_page4_note2_text: Characters can impose various Statuses on each other during the battle or have them from the very beginning of the battle. Make sure you familiarize yourself with them by hovering over the character.
main::status_third_eye_description: <color=#3cdcff>Restores [XN]<sprite name="insight"> insight</color> if the hero is in [Form] at the beginning of the turn.
main::form_mad_description: Provides +[N1]<sprite name="energy"> MAX energy, all damage<sprite name="damage"> to the character is increased by [N2]%.
main::combo_bonus_attack_text: By playing attacking cards, you increase the Attack Combo-Bonus, which will be added to all subsequent attacking cards in this turn.
main::combo_bonus_block_text: By playing blocking cards, you increase the Block Combo-Bonus, which will be added to all subsequent blocking cards in this turn.
main::combo_bonus_rage_text:
When the hero attacks enemies or when the hero is attacked, he gains <sprite name="berserker"> points.

Filling the meter grants the hero the <color=#ff40be>RAGE</color> status for 3 turns.
main::game_over_description_fuel:
Unfortunately, your car has run out of fuel, and there is no gas station nearby.

[name] won't be able to continue their journey. Getting stranded in this area is as good as death.
main::game_over_description_hp: Unfortunately, [name] has died from the injuries sustained. Their journey ends here.
main::game_over_description_sanity: Unfortunately, [name] has lost their mind from the horrors witnessed on their journey. Their journey ends here.
main::game_over_description_journey_end: Although [name] overcame all the obstacles in their path and fully completed their mission, they were unable to return to society. On the way back, [name] disappeared without a trace.
main::game_over_description_no_reason: Unfortunately, [name] perished under mysterious circumstances. Their journey has come to an end.
